HeaderUtility::isSpecial1xx(const ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) {\\n239:   return response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"100\\\" ||\\n240:          response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"102\\\" ||\\n241:          response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"103\\\" || response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"104\\\";\\n242: }\\n243: \\n244: bool HeaderUtility::isConnect(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n245:   return headers.Method() && headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect;\\n246: }\\n247: \\n248: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpRequest(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n249:   return headers.Upgrade() && absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(headers.getUpgradeValue(),\\n250:                                                      Http::Headers::get().UpgradeValues.ConnectUdp);\\n251: }\\n252: \\n253: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpResponse(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n254:   // In connect-udp case, Envoy will transform the H2 headers to H1 upgrade headers.\\n255:   // A valid response should have SwitchingProtocol status and connect-udp upgrade.\\n256:   return headers.Upgrade() && Utility::getResponseStatus(headers) == 101 &&\\n257:          absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(headers.getUpgradeValue(),\\n258:                                 Http::Headers::get().UpgradeValues.ConnectUdp);\\n259: }\\n260: \\n261: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectResponse(const RequestHeaderMap* request_headers,\\n262:                                       const ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) {\\n263:   return request_headers && isConnect(*request_headers) &&\\n264:          static_cast<Http::Code>(Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(response_headers)) ==\\n265:              Http::Code::OK;\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: bool HeaderUtility::rewriteAuthorityForConnectUdp(RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n269:   // Per RFC 9298, the URI template must only contain ASCII characters in the range 0x21-0x7E.\\n270:   absl::string_view path = headers.getPathValue();\\n271:   for (char c : path) {\\n272:     unsigned char ascii_code = static_cast<unsigned char>(c);\\n273:     if (ascii_code < 0x21 || ascii_code > 0x7e) {\\n274:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request with a bad character in the path {}\\\", path);\\n275:       return false;\\n276:     }\\n277:   }\\n278: \\n279:   // Extract target host and port from path using default template.\\n280:   if (!absl::StartsWith(path, \\\"/.well-known/masque/udp/\\\")) {\\n281:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request path is not a well-known URI: {}\\\", path);\\n282:     return false;\\n283:   }\\n284: \\n285:   std::vector<absl::string_view> path_split = absl::StrSplit(path, '/');\\n286:   if (path_split.size() != 7 || path_split[4].empty() || path_split[5].empty() ||\\n287:       !path_split[6].empty()) {\\n288:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request with a malformed URI template in the path {}\\\", path);\\n289:     return false;\\n290:   }\\n291: \\n292:   // Utility::PercentEncoding::decode never returns an empty string if the input argument is not\\n293:   // empty.\\n294:   std::string target_host = Utility::PercentEncoding::decode(path_split[4]);\\n295:   // Per RFC 9298, IPv6 Zone ID is not supported.\\n296:   if (target_host.find('%') != std::string::npos) {\\n297:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request with a non-escpaed char (%) in the path {}\\\", path);\\n298:     return false;\\n299:   }\\n300:   std::string target_port = Utility::PercentEncoding::decode(path_split[5]);\\n301: \\n302:   // If the host is an IPv6 address, surround the address with square brackets.\\n303:   in6_addr sin6_addr;\\n304:   bool is_ipv6 = (inet_pton(AF_INET6, target_host.c_str(), &sin6_addr) == 1);\\n305:   std::string new_host =\\n306:       absl::StrCat((is_ipv6 ? absl::StrCat(\\\"[\\\", target_host, \\\"]\\\") : target_host), \\\":\\\", target_port);\\n307:   headers.setHost(new_host);\\n308: \\n309:   return true;\\n310: }\\n311: \\n312: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_HTTP_DATAGRAMS\\n313: bool HeaderUtility::isCapsuleProtocol(const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n314:   Http::HeaderMap::GetResult capsule_protocol =\\n315:       headers.get(Envoy::Http::LowerCaseString(\\\"Capsule-Protocol\\\"));\\n316:   // When there are multiple Capsule-Protocol header entries, it returns false. RFC 9297 specifies\\n317:   // that non-boolean value types must be ignored. If there are multiple header entries, the value\\n318:   // type becomes a List so the header field must be ignored.\\n319:   if (capsule_protocol.size() != 1) {\\n320:     return false;\\n321:   }\\n322:   // Parses the header value and extracts the boolean value ignoring parameters.\\n323:   absl::optional<quiche::structured_headers::ParameterizedItem> header_item =\\n324:       quiche::structured_headers::ParseItem(capsule_protocol[0]->value().getStringView());\\n325:   return header_item && header_item->item.is_boolean() && header_item->item.GetBoolean();\\n326: }\\n327: #endif\\n328: \\n329: bool HeaderUtility::requestShouldHaveNoBody(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n330:   return (headers.Method() &&\\n331:           (headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Get ||\\n332:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Head ||\\n333:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Delete ||\\n334:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Trace ||\\n335:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect));\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: bool HeaderUtility::isEnvoyInternalRequest(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n339:   const HeaderEntry* internal_request_header = headers.EnvoyInternalRequest();\\n340:   return internal_request_header != nullptr &&\\n341:          internal_request_header->value() == Headers::get().EnvoyInternalRequestValues.True;\\n342: }\\n343: \\n344: void HeaderUtility::stripTrailingHostDot(RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n345:   auto host = headers.getHostValue();\\n346:   // If the host ends in a period, remove it.\\n347:   auto dot_index = host.rfind('.');\\n348:   if (dot_index == std::string::npos) {\\n349:     return;\\n350:   } else if (dot_index == (host.size() - 1)) {\\n351:     host.remove_suffix(1);\\n352:     headers.setHost(host);\\n353:     return;\\n354:   }\\n355:   // If the dot is just before a colon, it must be preceding the port number.\\n356:   // IPv6 addresses may contain colons or dots, but the dot will never directly\\n357:   // precede the colon, so this check should be sufficient to detect a trailing port number.\\n358:   if (host[dot_index + 1] == ':') {\\n359:     headers.setHost(absl::StrCat(host.substr(0, dot_index), host.substr(dot_index + 1)));\\n360:   }\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: bool HeaderUtility::hostHasPort(absl::string_view original_host) {\\n364:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(original_host);\\n365:   const absl::string_view port_str = original_host.substr(port_start + 1);\\n366:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n367:     return false;\\n368:   }\\n369:   uint32_t port = 0;\\n370:   if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(port_str, &port)) {\\n371:     return false;\\n372:   }\\n373:   return true;\\n374: }\\n375: \\n376: absl::optional<uint32_t> HeaderUtility::stripPortFromHost(RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n377:                                                           absl::optional<uint32_t> listener_port) {\\n378:   const absl::string_view original_host = headers.getHostValue();\\n379:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(original_host);\\n380:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n381:     return absl::nullopt;\\n382:   }\\n383:   const absl::string_view port_str = original_host.substr(port_start + 1);\\n384:   uint32_t port = 0;\\n385:   if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(port_str, &port)) {\\n386:     return absl::nullopt;\\n387:   }\\n388:   if (listener_port.has_value() && port != listener_port) {\\n389:     // We would strip ports only if it is specified and they are the same, as local port of the\\n390:     // listener.\\n391:     return absl::nullopt;\\n392:   }\\n393:   const absl::string_view host = original_host.substr(0, port_start);\\n394:   headers.setHost(host);\\n395:   return port;\\n396: }\\n397: \\n398: void HeaderUtility::stripPortFromHost(std::string& host) {\\n399:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(host);\\n400:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n401:     return;\\n402:   }\\n403:   host = host.substr(0, port_start);\\n404: }\\n405: \\n406: absl::string_view::size_type HeaderUtility::getPortStart(absl::string_view host) {\\n407:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = host.rfind(':');\\n408:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n409:     return absl::string_view::npos;\\n410:   }\\n411:   // According to RFC3986 v6 address is always enclosed in \\\"[]\\\". section 3.2.2.\\n412:   const auto v6_end_index = host.rfind(']');\\n413:   if (v6_end_index == absl::string_view::npos || v6_end_index < port_start) {\\n414:     if ((port_start + 1) > host.size()) {\\n415:       return absl::string_view::npos;\\n416:     }\\n417:     return port_start;\\n418:   }\\n419:   return absl::string_view::npos;\\n420: }\\n421: \\n422: constexpr bool isInvalidToken(unsigned char c) {\\n423:   if (c == '!' || c == '|' || c == '~' || c == '*' || c == '+' || c == '-' || c == '.' ||\\n424:       // #, $, %, &, '\\n425:       (c >= '#' && c <= '\\\\'') ||\\n426:       // [0-9]\\n427:       (c >= '0' && c <= '9') ||\\n428:       // [A-Z]\\n429:       (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') ||\\n430:       // ^, _, `, [a-z]\\n431:       (c >= '^' && c <= 'z')) {\\n432:     return false;\\n433:   }\\n434:   return true;\\n435: }\\n436: \\n437: absl::optional<std::reference_wrapper<const absl::string_view>>\\n438: HeaderUtility::requestHeadersValid(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n439:   // Make sure the host is valid.\\n440:   if (headers.Host() && !HeaderUtility::authorityIsValid(headers.Host()->value().getStringView())) {\\n441:     return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidAuthority;\\n442:   }\\n443:   if (headers.Method()) {\\n444:     absl::string_view method = headers.Method()->value().getStringView();\\n445:     if (method.empty() || std::any_of(method.begin(), method.end(), isInvalidToken)) {\\n446:       return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidMethod;\\n447:     }\\n448:   }\\n449:   if (headers.Scheme() && absl::StrContains(headers.Scheme()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\")) {\\n450:     return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidScheme;\\n451:   }\\n452:   return absl::nullopt;\\n453: }\\n454: \\n455: bool HeaderUtility::shouldCloseConnection(Http::Protocol protocol,\\n456:                                           const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n457:   // HTTP/1.0 defaults to single-use connections. Make sure the connection will be closed unless\\n458:   // Keep-Alive is present.\\n459:   if (protocol == Protocol::Http10 &&\\n460:       (!headers.Connection() ||\\n461:        !Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.Connection()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\",\\n462:                                          Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.KeepAlive))) {\\n463:     return true;\\n464:   }\\n465: \\n466:   if (protocol == Protocol::Http11 && headers.Connection() &&\\n467:       Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.Connection()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\",\\n468:                                        Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.Close)) {\\n469:     return true;\\n470:   }\\n471: \\n472:   // Note: Proxy-Connection is not a standard header, but is supported here\\n473:   // since it is supported by http-parser the underlying parser for http\\n474:   // requests.\\n475:   if (protocol < Protocol::Http2 && headers.ProxyConnection() &&\\n476:       Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.ProxyConnection()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\",\\n477:                                        Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.Close)) {\\n478:     return true;\\n479:   }\\n480:   return false;\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredRequestHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n484:   if (!headers.Method()) {\\n485:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n486:         absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Method.get()));\\n487:   }\\n488:   bool is_connect = Http::HeaderUtility::isConnect(headers);\\n489:   if (is_connect) {\\n490:     if (!headers.Host()) {\\n491:       // Host header must be present for CONNECT request.\\n492:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n493:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Host.get()));\\n494:     }\\n495:     if (headers.Path() && !headers.Protocol()) {\\n496:       // Path and Protocol header should only be present for CONNECT for upgrade style CONNECT.\\n497:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n498:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Protocol.get()));\\n499:     }\\n500:     if (!headers.Path() && headers.Protocol()) {\\n501:       // Path and Protocol header should only be present for CONNECT for upgrade style CONNECT.\\n502:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n503:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Path.get()));\\n504:     }\\n505:   } else {\\n506:     if (!headers.Path()) {\\n507:       // :path header must be present for non-CONNECT requests.\\n508:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n509:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Path.get()));\\n510:     }\\n511:   }\\n512:   return Http::okStatus();\\n513: }\\n514: \\n515: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkValidRequestHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n516:   if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\\\"envoy.reloadable_features.validate_upstream_headers\\\")) {\\n517:     return Http::okStatus();\\n518:   }\\n519: \\n520:   const HeaderEntry* invalid_entry = nullptr;\\n521:   bool invalid_key = false;\\n522:   headers.iterate([&invalid_entry, &invalid_key](const HeaderEntry& header) -> HeaderMap::Iterate {\\n523:     if (!HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(header.key().getStringView())) {\\n524:       invalid_entry = &header;\\n525:       invalid_key = true;\\n526:       return HeaderMap::Iterate::Break;\\n527:     }\\n528: \\n529:     if (!HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(header.value().getStringView())) {\\n530:       invalid_entry = &header;\\n531:       invalid_key = false;\\n532:       return HeaderMap::Iterate::Break;\\n533:     }\\n534: \\n535:     return HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\\n536:   });\\n537: \\n538:   if (invalid_entry) {\\n539:     // The header key may contain non-printable characters. Escape the key so that the error\\n540:     // details can be safely presented.\\n541:     const absl::string_view key = invalid_entry->key().getStringView();\\n542:     uint64_t extra_length = JsonEscaper::extraSpace(key);\\n543:     const std::string escaped_key = JsonEscaper::escapeString(key, extra_length);\\n544: \\n545:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n546:         absl::StrCat(\\\"invalid header \\\", invalid_key ? \\\"name: \\\" : \\\"value for: \\\", escaped_key));\\n547:   }\\n548:   return Http::okStatus();\\n549: }\\n550: \\n551: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(const Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n552:   const absl::optional<uint64_t> status = Utility::getResponseStatusOrNullopt(headers);\\n553:   if (!status.has_value()) {\\n554:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n555:         absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Status.get()));\\n556:   }\\n557:   return Http::okStatus();\\n558: }\\n559: \\n560: bool HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\\n561:   return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\\n562:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\\n563: }\\n564: \\n565: bool HeaderUtility::isModifiableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\\n566:   return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\\n567:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\\n568: }\\n569: \\n570: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult HeaderUtility::checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(\\n571:     absl::string_view header_name,\\n572:     envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\\n573:         headers_with_underscores_action,\\n574:     HeaderValidatorStats& stats) {\\n575:   if (headers_with_underscores_action == envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::ALLOW ||\\n576:       !HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(header_name)) {\\n577:     return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\\n578:   }\\n579:   if (headers_with_underscores_action ==\\n580:       envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::DROP_HEADER) {\\n581:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Dropping header with invalid characters in its name: {}\\\", header_name);\\n582:     stats.incDroppedHeadersWithUnderscores();\\n583:     return HeaderValidationResult::DROP;\\n584:   }\\n585:   ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Rejecting request due to header name with underscores: {}\\\", header_name);\\n586:   stats.incRequestsRejectedWithUnderscoresInHeaders();\\n587:   return HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n588: }\\n589: \\n590: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult\\n591: HeaderUtility::validateContentLength(absl::string_view header_value,\\n592:                                      bool override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message,\\n593:                                      bool& should_close_connection, size_t& content_length_output) {\\n594:   should_close_connection = false;\\n595:   std::vector<absl::string_view> values = absl::StrSplit(header_value, ',');\\n596:   absl::optional<uint64_t> content_length;\\n597:   for (const absl::string_view& value : values) {\\n598:     uint64_t new_value;\\n599:     if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(value, &new_value) ||\\n600:         !std::all_of(value.begin(), value.end(), absl::ascii_isdigit)) {\\n601:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Content length was either unparseable or negative\\\");\\n602:       should_close_connection = !override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message;\\n603:       return HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n604:     }\\n605:     if (!content_length.has_value()) {\\n606:       content_length = new_value;\\n607:       continue;\\n608:     }\\n609:     if (new_value != content_length.value()) {\\n610:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(\\n611:           debug,\\n612:           \\\"Parsed content length {} is inconsistent with previously detected content length {}\\\",\\n613:           new_value, content_length.value());\\n614:       should_close_connection = !override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message;\\n615:       return HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n616:     }\\n617:   }\\n618:   content_length_output = content_length.value();\\n619:   return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\\n620: }\\n621: \\n622: std::vector<absl::string_view>\\n623: HeaderUtility::parseCommaDelimitedHeader(absl::string_view header_value) {\\n624:   std::vector<absl::string_view> values;\\n625:   for (absl::string_view s : absl::StrSplit(header_value, ',')) {\\n626:     absl::string_view token = absl::StripAsciiWhitespace(s);\\n627:     if (token.empty()) {\\n628:       continue;\\n629:     }\\n630:     values.emplace_back(token);\\n631:   }\\n632:   return values;\\n633: }\\n634: \\n635: absl::string_view HeaderUtility::getSemicolonDelimitedAttribute(absl::string_view value) {\\n636:   return absl::StripAsciiWhitespace(StringUtil::cropRight(value, \\\";\\\"));\\n637: }\\n638: \\n639: std::string HeaderUtility::addEncodingToAcceptEncoding(absl::string_view accept_encoding_header,\\n640:                                                        absl::string_view encoding) {\\n641:   // Append the content encoding only if it isn't already present in the\\n642:   // accept_encoding header. If it is present with a q-value (\\\"gzip;q=0.3\\\"),\\n643:   // remove the q-value to indicate that the content encoding setting that we\\n644:   // add has max priority (i.e. q-value 1.0).\\n645:   std::vector<absl::string_view> newContentEncodings;\\n646:   std::vector<absl::string_view> contentEncodings =\\n647:       Http::HeaderUtility::parseCommaDelimitedHeader(accept_encoding_header);\\n648:   for (absl::string_view contentEncoding : contentEncodings) {\\n649:     absl::string_view strippedEncoding =\\n650:         Http::HeaderUtility::getSemicolonDelimitedAttribute(contentEncoding);\\n651:     if (strippedEncoding != encoding) {\\n652:       // Add back all content encodings back except for the content encoding that we want to\\n653:       // add. For example, if content encoding is \\\"gzip\\\", this filters out encodings \\\"gzip\\\" and\\n654:       // \\\"gzip;q=0.6\\\".\\n655:       newContentEncodings.push_back(contentEncoding);\\n656:     }\\n657:   }\\n658:   // Finally add a single instance of our content encoding.\\n659:   newContentEncodings.push_back(encoding);\\n660:   return absl::StrJoin(newContentEncodings, \\\",\\\");\\n661: }\\n662: \\n663: bool HeaderUtility::isStandardConnectRequest(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n664:   return headers.getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect &&\\n665:          headers.getProtocolValue().empty();\\n666: }\\n667: \\n668: bool HeaderUtility::isExtendedH2ConnectRequest(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n669:   return headers.getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect &&\\n670:          !headers.getProtocolValue().empty();\\n671: }\\n672: \\n673: bool HeaderUtility::isPseudoHeader(absl::string_view header_name) {\\n674:   return !header_name.empty() && header_name[0] == ':';\\n675: }\\n676: \\n677: } // namespace Http\\n678: } // namespace Envoy\\n679: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/header_utility.cc\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #include \"source/common/http/header_utility.h\"\n2: \n3: #include \"envoy/config/route/v3/route_components.pb.h\"\n4: \n5: #include \"source/common/common/json_escape_string.h\"\n6: #include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n7: #include \"source/common/common/regex.h\"\n8: #include \"source/common/common/utility.h\"\n9: #include \"source/common/http/character_set_validation.h\"\n10: #include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n11: #include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n12: #include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n13: #include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n14: \n15: #include \"absl/strings/match.h\"\n16: \n17: #ifdef ENVOY_NGHTTP2\n18: #include \"nghttp2/nghttp2.h\"\n19: #endif\n20: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_HTTP_DATAGRAMS\n21: #include \"quiche/common/structured_headers.h\"\n22: #endif\n23: #include \"quiche/http2/adapter/header_validator.h\"\n24: \n25: namespace Envoy {\n26: namespace Http {\n27: \n28: struct SharedResponseCodeDetailsValues {\n29:   const absl::string_view InvalidAuthority = \"http.invalid_authority\";\n30:   const absl::string_view ConnectUnsupported = \"http.connect_not_supported\";\n31:   const absl::string_view InvalidMethod = \"http.invalid_method\";\n32:   const absl::string_view InvalidPath = \"http.invalid_path\";\n33:   const absl::string_view InvalidScheme = \"http.invalid_scheme\";\n34: };\n35: \n36: using SharedResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<SharedResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\n37: \n38: bool HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(const HeaderMap& request_headers,\n39:                                  const std::vector<HeaderDataPtr>& config_headers) {\n40:   // No headers to match is considered a match.\n41:   if (!config_headers.empty()) {\n42:     for (const HeaderDataPtr& cfg_header_data : config_headers) {\n43:       if (!cfg_header_data->matchesHeaders(request_headers)) {\n44:         return false;\n45:       }\n46:     }\n47:   }\n48: \n49:   return true;\n50: }\n51: \n52: HeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\n53: HeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap::GetResult& header_value,\n54:                                       absl::string_view separator) {\n55:   GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\n56:   // In this case we concatenate all found headers using a delimiter before performing the\n57:   // final match. We use an InlinedVector of absl::string_view to invoke the optimized join\n58:   // algorithm. This requires a copying phase before we invoke join. The 3 used as the inline\n59:   // size has been arbitrarily chosen.\n60:   // TODO(mattklein123): Do we need to normalize any whitespace here?\n61:   absl::InlinedVector<absl::string_view, 3> string_view_vector;\n62:   string_view_vector.reserve(header_value.size());\n63:   for (size_t i = 0; i < header_value.size(); i++) {\n64:     string_view_vector.push_back(header_value[i]->value().getStringView());\n65:   }\n66:   result.result_backing_string_ = absl::StrJoin(string_view_vector, separator);\n67: \n68:   return result;\n69: }\n70: \n71: HeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\n72: HeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap& headers, const Http::LowerCaseString& key,\n73:                                       absl::string_view separator) {\n74:   GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\n75:   const auto header_value = headers.get(key);\n76: \n77:   if (header_value.empty()) {\n78:     // Empty for clarity. Avoid handling the empty case in the block below if the runtime feature\n79:     // is disabled.\n80:   } else if (header_value.size() == 1) {\n81:     result.result_ = header_value[0]->value().getStringView();\n82:   } else {\n83:     return getAllOfHeaderAsString(header_value, separator);\n84:   }\n85: \n86:   return result;\n87: }\n88: \n89: HeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr\n90: HeaderUtility::createHeaderData(const envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher& config,\n91:                                 Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context) {\n92:   switch (config.header_match_specifier_case()) {\n93:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kExactMatch:\n94:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataExactMatch>(config);\n95:     break;\n96:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kSafeRegexMatch:\n97:     return THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(HeaderDataRegexMatch::create(config, factory_context),\n98:                                  std::unique_ptr<HeaderDataRegexMatch>);\n99:     break;\n100:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kRangeMatch:\n101:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataRangeMatch>(config);\n102:     break;\n103:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kPresentMatch:\n104:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataPresentMatch>(config);\n105:     break;\n106:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kPrefixMatch:\n107:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataPrefixMatch>(config);\n108:     break;\n109:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kSuffixMatch:\n110:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataSuffixMatch>(config);\n111:     break;\n112:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kContainsMatch:\n113:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataContainsMatch>(config);\n114:     break;\n115:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kStringMatch:\n116:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataStringMatch>(config, factory_context);\n117:     break;\n118:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::\n119:       HEADER_MATCH_SPECIFIER_NOT_SET:\n120:     FALLTHRU;\n121:   default:\n122:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataPresentMatch>(config, true);\n123:     break;\n124:   }\n125: }\n126: \n127: bool HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(const absl::string_view header_value) {\n128:   return http2::adapter::HeaderValidator::IsValidHeaderValue(header_value,\n129:                                                              http2::adapter::ObsTextOption::kAllow);\n130: }\n131: \n132: bool HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(absl::string_view header_key) {\n133:   if (!header_key.empty() && header_key[0] == ':') {\n134:     header_key.remove_prefix(1);\n135:     if (header_key.empty()) {\n136:       return false;\n137:     }\n138:   }\n139:   // For all other header use HTTP/1 semantics. The only difference from HTTP/2 is that\n140:   // uppercase characters are allowed. This allows HTTP filters to add header with mixed\n141:   // case names. The HTTP/1 codec will send as is, as uppercase characters are allowed.\n142:   // However the HTTP/2 codec will NOT convert these to lowercase when serializing the\n143:   // header map, thus producing an invalid request.\n144:   // TODO(yanavlasov): make validation in HTTP/2 case stricter.\n145:   bool is_valid = true;\n146:   for (auto iter = header_key.begin(); iter != header_key.end() && is_valid; ++iter) {\n147:     is_valid &= testCharInTable(kGenericHeaderNameCharTable, *iter);\n148:   }\n149:   return is_valid;\n150: }\n151: \n152: bool HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(const absl::string_view header_name) {\n153:   return header_name.find('_') != absl::string_view::npos;\n154: }\n155: \n156: bool HeaderUtility::authorityIsValid(const absl::string_view header_value) {\n157:   // This function validates the authority header for both HTTP/1 and HTTP/2.\n158:   // Note the HTTP/1 spec allows \"user-info@host:port\" for the authority, whereas\n159:   // the HTTP/2 spec only allows \"host:port\". Thus, this function permits all the\n160:   // HTTP/2 valid characters (similar to oghttp2's implementation) and the \"@\" character.\n161:   // Once UHV is used, this function should be removed, and the HTTP/1 and HTTP/2\n162:   // authority validations should be different.\n163:   static constexpr char ValidAuthorityChars[] = {\n164:       0 /* NUL  */, 0 /* SOH  */, 0 /* STX  */, 0 /* ETX  */,\n165:       0 /* EOT  */, 0 /* ENQ  */, 0 /* ACK  */, 0 /* BEL  */,\n166:       0 /* BS   */, 0 /* HT   */, 0 /* LF   */, 0 /* VT   */,\n167:       0 /* FF   */, 0 /* CR   */, 0 /* SO   */, 0 /* SI   */,\n168:       0 /* DLE  */, 0 /* DC1  */, 0 /* DC2  */, 0 /* DC3  */,\n169:       0 /* DC4  */, 0 /* NAK  */, 0 /* SYN  */, 0 /* ETB  */,\n170:       0 /* CAN  */, 0 /* EM   */, 0 /* SUB  */, 0 /* ESC  */,\n171:       0 /* FS   */, 0 /* GS   */, 0 /* RS   */, 0 /* US   */,\n172:       0 /* SPC  */, 1 /* !    */, 0 /* \"    */, 0 /* #    */,\n173:       1 /* $    */, 1 /* %    */, 1 /* &    */, 1 /* '    */,\n174:       1 /* (    */, 1 /* )    */, 1 /* *    */, 1 /* +    */,\n175:       1 /* ,    */, 1 /* -    */, 1 /* . */,    0 /* /    */,\n176:       1 /* 0    */, 1 /* 1    */, 1 /* 2    */, 1 /* 3    */,\n177:       1 /* 4    */, 1 /* 5    */, 1 /* 6    */, 1 /* 7    */,\n178:       1 /* 8    */, 1 /* 9    */, 1 /* :    */, 1 /* ;    */,\n179:       0 /* <    */, 1 /* =    */, 0 /* >    */, 0 /* ?    */,\n180:       1 /* @    */, 1 /* A    */, 1 /* B    */, 1 /* C    */,\n181:       1 /* D    */, 1 /* E    */, 1 /* F    */, 1 /* G    */,\n182:       1 /* H    */, 1 /* I    */, 1 /* J    */, 1 /* K    */,\n183:       1 /* L    */, 1 /* M    */, 1 /* N    */, 1 /* O    */,\n184:       1 /* P    */, 1 /* Q    */, 1 /* R    */, 1 /* S    */,\n185:       1 /* T    */, 1 /* U    */, 1 /* V    */, 1 /* W    */,\n186:       1 /* X    */, 1 /* Y    */, 1 /* Z    */, 1 /* [    */,\n187:       0 /* \\    */, 1 /* ]    */, 0 /* ^    */, 1 /* _    */,\n188:       0 /* `    */, 1 /* a    */, 1 /* b    */, 1 /* c    */,\n189:       1 /* d    */, 1 /* e    */, 1 /* f    */, 1 /* g    */,\n190:       1 /* h    */, 1 /* i    */, 1 /* j    */, 1 /* k    */,\n191:       1 /* l    */, 1 /* m    */, 1 /* n    */, 1 /* o    */,\n192:       1 /* p    */, 1 /* q    */, 1 /* r    */, 1 /* s    */,\n193:       1 /* t    */, 1 /* u    */, 1 /* v    */, 1 /* w    */,\n194:       1 /* x    */, 1 /* y    */, 1 /* z    */, 0 /* {    */,\n195:       0 /* |    */, 0 /* }    */, 1 /* ~    */, 0 /* DEL  */,\n196:       0 /* 0x80 */, 0 /* 0x81 */, 0 /* 0x82 */, 0 /* 0x83 */,\n197:       0 /* 0x84 */, 0 /* 0x85 */, 0 /* 0x86 */, 0 /* 0x87 */,\n198:       0 /* 0x88 */, 0 /* 0x89 */, 0 /* 0x8a */, 0 /* 0x8b */,\n199:       0 /* 0x8c */, 0 /* 0x8d */, 0 /* 0x8e */, 0 /* 0x8f */,\n200:       0 /* 0x90 */, 0 /* 0x91 */, 0 /* 0x92 */, 0 /* 0x93 */,\n201:       0 /* 0x94 */, 0 /* 0x95 */, 0 /* 0x96 */, 0 /* 0x97 */,\n202:       0 /* 0x98 */, 0 /* 0x99 */, 0 /* 0x9a */, 0 /* 0x9b */,\n203:       0 /* 0x9c */, 0 /* 0x9d */, 0 /* 0x9e */, 0 /* 0x9f */,\n204:       0 /* 0xa0 */, 0 /* 0xa1 */, 0 /* 0xa2 */, 0 /* 0xa3 */,\n205:       0 /* 0xa4 */, 0 /* 0xa5 */, 0 /* 0xa6 */, 0 /* 0xa7 */,\n206:       0 /* 0xa8 */, 0 /* 0xa9 */, 0 /* 0xaa */, 0 /* 0xab */,\n207:       0 /* 0xac */, 0 /* 0xad */, 0 /* 0xae */, 0 /* 0xaf */,\n208:       0 /* 0xb0 */, 0 /* 0xb1 */, 0 /* 0xb2 */, 0 /* 0xb3 */,\n209:       0 /* 0xb4 */, 0 /* 0xb5 */, 0 /* 0xb6 */, 0 /* 0xb7 */,\n210:       0 /* 0xb8 */, 0 /* 0xb9 */, 0 /* 0xba */, 0 /* 0xbb */,\n211:       0 /* 0xbc */, 0 /* 0xbd */, 0 /* 0xbe */, 0 /* 0xbf */,\n212:       0 /* 0xc0 */, 0 /* 0xc1 */, 0 /* 0xc2 */, 0 /* 0xc3 */,\n213:       0 /* 0xc4 */, 0 /* 0xc5 */, 0 /* 0xc6 */, 0 /* 0xc7 */,\n214:       0 /* 0xc8 */, 0 /* 0xc9 */, 0 /* 0xca */, 0 /* 0xcb */,\n215:       0 /* 0xcc */, 0 /* 0xcd */, 0 /* 0xce */, 0 /* 0xcf */,\n216:       0 /* 0xd0 */, 0 /* 0xd1 */, 0 /* 0xd2 */, 0 /* 0xd3 */,\n217:       0 /* 0xd4 */, 0 /* 0xd5 */, 0 /* 0xd6 */, 0 /* 0xd7 */,\n218:       0 /* 0xd8 */, 0 /* 0xd9 */, 0 /* 0xda */, 0 /* 0xdb */,\n219:       0 /* 0xdc */, 0 /* 0xdd */, 0 /* 0xde */, 0 /* 0xdf */,\n220:       0 /* 0xe0 */, 0 /* 0xe1 */, 0 /* 0xe2 */, 0 /* 0xe3 */,\n221:       0 /* 0xe4 */, 0 /* 0xe5 */, 0 /* 0xe6 */, 0 /* 0xe7 */,\n222:       0 /* 0xe8 */, 0 /* 0xe9 */, 0 /* 0xea */, 0 /* 0xeb */,\n223:       0 /* 0xec */, 0 /* 0xed */, 0 /* 0xee */, 0 /* 0xef */,\n224:       0 /* 0xf0 */, 0 /* 0xf1 */, 0 /* 0xf2 */, 0 /* 0xf3 */,\n225:       0 /* 0xf4 */, 0 /* 0xf5 */, 0 /* 0xf6 */, 0 /* 0xf7 */,\n226:       0 /* 0xf8 */, 0 /* 0xf9 */, 0 /* 0xfa */, 0 /* 0xfb */,\n227:       0 /* 0xfc */, 0 /* 0xfd */, 0 /* 0xfe */, 0 /* 0xff */\n228:   };\n229: \n230:   for (const uint8_t c : header_value) {\n231:     if (!ValidAuthorityChars[c]) {\n232:       return false;\n233:     }\n234:   }\n235:   return true;\n236: }\n237: \n238: bool HeaderUtility::isSpecial1xx(const ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) {\n239:   return response_headers.Status()->value() == \"100\" ||\n240:          response_headers.Status()->value() == \"102\" ||\n241:          response_headers.Status()->value() == \"103\" || response_headers.Status()->value() == \"104\";\n242: }\n243: \n244: bool HeaderUtility::isConnect(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n245:   return headers.Method() && headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect;\n246: }\n247: \n248: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpRequest(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n249:   return headers.Upgrade() && absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(headers.getUpgradeValue(),\n250:                                                      Http::Headers::get().UpgradeValues.ConnectUdp);\n251: }\n252: \n253: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpResponse(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n254:   // In connect-udp case, Envoy will transform the H2 headers to H1 upgrade headers.\n255:   // A valid response should have SwitchingProtocol status and connect-udp upgrade.\n256:   return headers.Upgrade() && Utility::getResponseStatus(headers) == 101 &&\n257:          absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(headers.getUpgradeValue(),\n258:                                 Http::Headers::get().UpgradeValues.ConnectUdp);\n259: }\n260: \n261: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectResponse(const RequestHeaderMap* request_headers,\n262:                                       const ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) {\n263:   return request_headers && isConnect(*request_headers) &&\n264:          static_cast<Http::Code>(Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(response_headers)) ==\n265:              Http::Code::OK;\n266: }\n267: \n268: bool HeaderUtility::rewriteAuthorityForConnectUdp(RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n269:   // Per RFC 9298, the URI template must only contain ASCII characters in the range 0x21-0x7E.\n270:   absl::string_view path = headers.getPathValue();\n271:   for (char c : path) {\n272:     unsigned char ascii_code = static_cast<unsigned char>(c);\n273:     if (ascii_code < 0x21 || ascii_code > 0x7e) {\n274:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \"CONNECT-UDP request with a bad character in the path {}\", path);\n275:       return false;\n276:     }\n277:   }\n278: \n279:   // Extract target host and port from path using default template.\n280:   if (!absl::StartsWith(path, \"/.well-known/masque/udp/\")) {\n281:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \"CONNECT-UDP request path is not a well-known URI: {}\", path);\n282:     return false;\n283:   }\n284: \n285:   std::vector<absl::string_view> path_split = absl::StrSplit(path, '/');\n286:   if (path_split.size() != 7 || path_split[4].empty() || path_split[5].empty() ||\n287:       !path_split[6].empty()) {\n288:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \"CONNECT-UDP request with a malformed URI template in the path {}\", path);\n289:     return false;\n290:   }\n291: \n292:   // Utility::PercentEncoding::decode never returns an empty string if the input argument is not\n293:   // empty.\n294:   std::string target_host = Utility::PercentEncoding::decode(path_split[4]);\n295:   // Per RFC 9298, IPv6 Zone ID is not supported.\n296:   if (target_host.find('%') != std::string::npos) {\n297:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \"CONNECT-UDP request with a non-escpaed char (%) in the path {}\", path);\n298:     return false;\n299:   }\n300:   std::string target_port = Utility::PercentEncoding::decode(path_split[5]);\n301: \n302:   // If the host is an IPv6 address, surround the address with square brackets.\n303:   in6_addr sin6_addr;\n304:   bool is_ipv6 = (inet_pton(AF_INET6, target_host.c_str(), &sin6_addr) == 1);\n305:   std::string new_host =\n306:       absl::StrCat((is_ipv6 ? absl::StrCat(\"[\", target_host, \"]\") : target_host), \":\", target_port);\n307:   headers.setHost(new_host);\n308: \n309:   return true;\n310: }\n311: \n312: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_HTTP_DATAGRAMS\n313: bool HeaderUtility::isCapsuleProtocol(const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n314:   Http::HeaderMap::GetResult capsule_protocol =\n315:       headers.get(Envoy::Http::LowerCaseString(\"Capsule-Protocol\"));\n316:   // When there are multiple Capsule-Protocol header entries, it returns false. RFC 9297 specifies\n317:   // that non-boolean value types must be ignored. If there are multiple header entries, the value\n318:   // type becomes a List so the header field must be ignored.\n319:   if (capsule_protocol.size() != 1) {\n320:     return false;\n321:   }\n322:   // Parses the header value and extracts the boolean value ignoring parameters.\n323:   absl::optional<quiche::structured_headers::ParameterizedItem> header_item =\n324:       quiche::structured_headers::ParseItem(capsule_protocol[0]->value().getStringView());\n325:   return header_item && header_item->item.is_boolean() && header_item->item.GetBoolean();\n326: }\n327: #endif\n328: \n329: bool HeaderUtility::requestShouldHaveNoBody(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n330:   return (headers.Method() &&\n331:           (headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Get ||\n332:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Head ||\n333:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Delete ||\n334:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Trace ||\n335:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect));\n336: }\n337: \n338: bool HeaderUtility::isEnvoyInternalRequest(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n339:   const HeaderEntry* internal_request_header = headers.EnvoyInternalRequest();\n340:   return internal_request_header != nullptr &&\n341:          internal_request_header->value() == Headers::get().EnvoyInternalRequestValues.True;\n342: }\n343: \n344: void HeaderUtility::stripTrailingHostDot(RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n345:   auto host = headers.getHostValue();\n346:   // If the host ends in a period, remove it.\n347:   auto dot_index = host.rfind('.');\n348:   if (dot_index == std::string::npos) {\n349:     return;\n350:   } else if (dot_index == (host.size() - 1)) {\n351:     host.remove_suffix(1);\n352:     headers.setHost(host);\n353:     return;\n354:   }\n355:   // If the dot is just before a colon, it must be preceding the port number.\n356:   // IPv6 addresses may contain colons or dots, but the dot will never directly\n357:   // precede the colon, so this check should be sufficient to detect a trailing port number.\n358:   if (host[dot_index + 1] == ':') {\n359:     headers.setHost(absl::StrCat(host.substr(0, dot_index), host.substr(dot_index + 1)));\n360:   }\n361: }\n362: \n363: bool HeaderUtility::hostHasPort(absl::string_view original_host) {\n364:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(original_host);\n365:   const absl::string_view port_str = original_host.substr(port_start + 1);\n366:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\n367:     return false;\n368:   }\n369:   uint32_t port = 0;\n370:   if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(port_str, &port)) {\n371:     return false;\n372:   }\n373:   return true;\n374: }\n375: \n376: absl::optional<uint32_t> HeaderUtility::stripPortFromHost(RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n377:                                                           absl::optional<uint32_t> listener_port) {\n378:   const absl::string_view original_host = headers.getHostValue();\n379:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(original_host);\n380:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\n381:     return absl::nullopt;\n382:   }\n383:   const absl::string_view port_str = original_host.substr(port_start + 1);\n384:   uint32_t port = 0;\n385:   if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(port_str, &port)) {\n386:     return absl::nullopt;\n387:   }\n388:   if (listener_port.has_value() && port != listener_port) {\n389:     // We would strip ports only if it is specified and they are the same, as local port of the\n390:     // listener.\n391:     return absl::nullopt;\n392:   }\n393:   const absl::string_view host = original_host.substr(0, port_start);\n394:   headers.setHost(host);\n395:   return port;\n396: }\n397: \n398: void HeaderUtility::stripPortFromHost(std::string& host) {\n399:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(host);\n400:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\n401:     return;\n402:   }\n403:   host = host.substr(0, port_start);\n404: }\n405: \n406: absl::string_view::size_type HeaderUtility::getPortStart(absl::string_view host) {\n407:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = host.rfind(':');\n408:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\n409:     return absl::string_view::npos;\n410:   }\n411:   // According to RFC3986 v6 address is always enclosed in \"[]\". section 3.2.2.\n412:   const auto v6_end_index = host.rfind(']');\n413:   if (v6_end_index == absl::string_view::npos || v6_end_index < port_start) {\n414:     if ((port_start + 1) > host.size()) {\n415:       return absl::string_view::npos;\n416:     }\n417:     return port_start;\n418:   }\n419:   return absl::string_view::npos;\n420: }\n421: \n422: constexpr bool isInvalidToken(unsigned char c) {\n423:   if (c == '!' || c == '|' || c == '~' || c == '*' || c == '+' || c == '-' || c == '.' ||\n424:       // #, $, %, &, '\n425:       (c >= '#' && c <= '\\'') ||\n426:       // [0-9]\n427:       (c >= '0' && c <= '9') ||\n428:       // [A-Z]\n429:       (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') ||\n430:       // ^, _, `, [a-z]\n431:       (c >= '^' && c <= 'z')) {\n432:     return false;\n433:   }\n434:   return true;\n435: }\n436: \n437: absl::optional<std::reference_wrapper<const absl::string_view>>\n438: HeaderUtility::requestHeadersValid(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n439:   // Make sure the host is valid.\n440:   if (headers.Host() && !HeaderUtility::authorityIsValid(headers.Host()->value().getStringView())) {\n441:     return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidAuthority;\n442:   }\n443:   if (headers.Method()) {\n444:     absl::string_view method = headers.Method()->value().getStringView();\n445:     if (method.empty() || std::any_of(method.begin(), method.end(), isInvalidToken)) {\n446:       return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidMethod;\n447:     }\n448:   }\n449:   if (headers.Scheme() && absl::StrContains(headers.Scheme()->value().getStringView(), \",\")) {\n450:     return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidScheme;\n451:   }\n452:   return absl::nullopt;\n453: }\n454: \n455: bool HeaderUtility::shouldCloseConnection(Http::Protocol protocol,\n456:                                           const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n457:   // HTTP/1.0 defaults to single-use connections. Make sure the connection will be closed unless\n458:   // Keep-Alive is present.\n459:   if (protocol == Protocol::Http10 &&\n460:       (!headers.Connection() ||\n461:        !Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.Connection()->value().getStringView(), \",\",\n462:                                          Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.KeepAlive))) {\n463:     return true;\n464:   }\n465: \n466:   if (protocol == Protocol::Http11 && headers.Connection() &&\n467:       Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.Connection()->value().getStringView(), \",\",\n468:                                        Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.Close)) {\n469:     return true;\n470:   }\n471: \n472:   // Note: Proxy-Connection is not a standard header, but is supported here\n473:   // since it is supported by http-parser the underlying parser for http\n474:   // requests.\n475:   if (protocol < Protocol::Http2 && headers.ProxyConnection() &&\n476:       Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.ProxyConnection()->value().getStringView(), \",\",\n477:                                        Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.Close)) {\n478:     return true;\n479:   }\n480:   return false;\n481: }\n482: \n483: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredRequestHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n484:   if (!headers.Method()) {\n485:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n486:         absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Method.get()));\n487:   }\n488:   bool is_connect = Http::HeaderUtility::isConnect(headers);\n489:   if (is_connect) {\n490:     if (!headers.Host()) {\n491:       // Host header must be present for CONNECT request.\n492:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n493:           absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Host.get()));\n494:     }\n495:     if (headers.Path() && !headers.Protocol()) {\n496:       // Path and Protocol header should only be present for CONNECT for upgrade style CONNECT.\n497:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n498:           absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Protocol.get()));\n499:     }\n500:     if (!headers.Path() && headers.Protocol()) {\n501:       // Path and Protocol header should only be present for CONNECT for upgrade style CONNECT.\n502:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n503:           absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Path.get()));\n504:     }\n505:   } else {\n506:     if (!headers.Path()) {\n507:       // :path header must be present for non-CONNECT requests.\n508:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n509:           absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Path.get()));\n510:     }\n511:   }\n512:   return Http::okStatus();\n513: }\n514: \n515: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkValidRequestHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n516:   if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\"envoy.reloadable_features.validate_upstream_headers\")) {\n517:     return Http::okStatus();\n518:   }\n519: \n520:   const HeaderEntry* invalid_entry = nullptr;\n521:   bool invalid_key = false;\n522:   headers.iterate([&invalid_entry, &invalid_key](const HeaderEntry& header) -> HeaderMap::Iterate {\n523:     if (!HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(header.key().getStringView())) {\n524:       invalid_entry = &header;\n525:       invalid_key = true;\n526:       return HeaderMap::Iterate::Break;\n527:     }\n528: \n529:     if (!HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(header.value().getStringView())) {\n530:       invalid_entry = &header;\n531:       invalid_key = false;\n532:       return HeaderMap::Iterate::Break;\n533:     }\n534: \n535:     return HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n536:   });\n537: \n538:   if (invalid_entry) {\n539:     // The header key may contain non-printable characters. Escape the key so that the error\n540:     // details can be safely presented.\n541:     const absl::string_view key = invalid_entry->key().getStringView();\n542:     uint64_t extra_length = JsonEscaper::extraSpace(key);\n543:     const std::string escaped_key = JsonEscaper::escapeString(key, extra_length);\n544: \n545:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n546:         absl::StrCat(\"invalid header \", invalid_key ? \"name: \" : \"value for: \", escaped_key));\n547:   }\n548:   return Http::okStatus();\n549: }\n550: \n551: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(const Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n552:   const absl::optional<uint64_t> status = Utility::getResponseStatusOrNullopt(headers);\n553:   if (!status.has_value()) {\n554:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n555:         absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Status.get()));\n556:   }\n557:   return Http::okStatus();\n558: }\n559: \n560: bool HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\n561:   return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\n562:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\n563: }\n564: \n565: bool HeaderUtility::isModifiableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\n566:   return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\n567:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\n568: }\n569: \n570: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult HeaderUtility::checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(\n571:     absl::string_view header_name,\n572:     envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n573:         headers_with_underscores_action,\n574:     HeaderValidatorStats& stats) {\n575:   if (headers_with_underscores_action == envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::ALLOW ||\n576:       !HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(header_name)) {\n577:     return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\n578:   }\n579:   if (headers_with_underscores_action ==\n580:       envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::DROP_HEADER) {\n581:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \"Dropping header with invalid characters in its name: {}\", header_name);\n582:     stats.incDroppedHeadersWithUnderscores();\n583:     return HeaderValidationResult::DROP;\n584:   }\n585:   ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \"Rejecting request due to header name with underscores: {}\", header_name);\n586:   stats.incRequestsRejectedWithUnderscoresInHeaders();\n587:   return HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\n588: }\n589: \n590: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult\n591: HeaderUtility::validateContentLength(absl::string_view header_value,\n592:                                      bool override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message,\n593:                                      bool& should_close_connection, size_t& content_length_output) {\n594:   should_close_connection = false;\n595:   std::vector<absl::string_view> values = absl::StrSplit(header_value, ',');\n596:   absl::optional<uint64_t> content_length;\n597:   for (const absl::string_view& value : values) {\n598:     uint64_t new_value;\n599:     if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(value, &new_value) ||\n600:         !std::all_of(value.begin(), value.end(), absl::ascii_isdigit)) {\n601:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \"Content length was either unparseable or negative\");\n602:       should_close_connection = !override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message;\n603:       return HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\n604:     }\n605:     if (!content_length.has_value()) {\n606:       content_length = new_value;\n607:       continue;\n608:     }\n609:     if (new_value != content_length.value()) {\n610:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(\n611:           debug,\n612:           \"Parsed content length {} is inconsistent with previously detected content length {}\",\n613:           new_value, content_length.value());\n614:       should_close_connection = !override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message;\n615:       return HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\n616:     }\n617:   }\n618:   content_length_output = content_length.value();\n619:   return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\n620: }\n621: \n622: std::vector<absl::string_view>\n623: HeaderUtility::parseCommaDelimitedHeader(absl::string_view header_value) {\n624:   std::vector<absl::string_view> values;\n625:   for (absl::string_view s : absl::StrSplit(header_value, ',')) {\n626:     absl::string_view token = absl::StripAsciiWhitespace(s);\n627:     if (token.empty()) {\n628:       continue;\n629:     }\n630:     values.emplace_back(token);\n631:   }\n632:   return values;\n633: }\n634: \n635: absl::string_view HeaderUtility::getSemicolonDelimitedAttribute(absl::string_view value) {\n636:   return absl::StripAsciiWhitespace(StringUtil::cropRight(value, \";\"));\n637: }\n638: \n639: std::string HeaderUtility::addEncodingToAcceptEncoding(absl::string_view accept_encoding_header,\n640:                                                        absl::string_view encoding) {\n641:   // Append the content encoding only if it isn't already present in the\n642:   // accept_encoding header. If it is present with a q-value (\"gzip;q=0.3\"),\n643:   // remove the q-value to indicate that the content encoding setting that we\n644:   // add has max priority (i.e. q-value 1.0).\n645:   std::vector<absl::string_view> newContentEncodings;\n646:   std::vector<absl::string_view> contentEncodings =\n647:       Http::HeaderUtility::parseCommaDelimitedHeader(accept_encoding_header);\n648:   for (absl::string_view contentEncoding : contentEncodings) {\n649:     absl::string_view strippedEncoding =\n650:         Http::HeaderUtility::getSemicolonDelimitedAttribute(contentEncoding);\n651:     if (strippedEncoding != encoding) {\n652:       // Add back all content encodings back except for the content encoding that we want to\n653:       // add. For example, if content encoding is \"gzip\", this filters out encodings \"gzip\" and\n654:       // \"gzip;q=0.6\".\n655:       newContentEncodings.push_back(contentEncoding);\n656:     }\n657:   }\n658:   // Finally add a single instance of our content encoding.\n659:   newContentEncodings.push_back(encoding);\n660:   return absl::StrJoin(newContentEncodings, \",\");\n661: }\n662: \n663: bool HeaderUtility::isStandardConnectRequest(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n664:   return headers.getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect &&\n665:          headers.getProtocolValue().empty();\n666: }\n667: \n668: bool HeaderUtility::isExtendedH2ConnectRequest(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n669:   return headers.getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect &&\n670:          !headers.getProtocolValue().empty();\n671: }\n672: \n673: bool HeaderUtility::isPseudoHeader(absl::string_view header_name) {\n674:   return !header_name.empty() && header_name[0] == ':';\n675: }\n676: \n677: } // namespace Http\n678: } // namespace Envoy\n679: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/header_utility.cc"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: #include \\\"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\\\"\\n2: \\n3: #include <functional>\\n4: \\n5: #include \\\"envoy/http/header_map.h\\\"\\n6: #include \\\"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\\\"\\n7: \\n8: #include \\\"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\\\"\\n9: #include \\\"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\\\"\\n10: #include \\\"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\\\"\\n11: #include \\\"source/common/http/codes.h\\\"\\n12: #include \\\"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\\\"\\n13: #include \\\"source/common/http/header_utility.h\\\"\\n14: #include \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n15: #include \\\"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\\\"\\n16: \\n17: #include \\\"matching/data_impl.h\\\"\\n18: \\n19: namespace Envoy {\\n20: namespace Http {\\n21: \\n22: namespace {\\n23: \\n24: // Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\\n25: template <class Filters>\\n26: void recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\\n27:                             typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\\n28:   // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\\n29:   if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\\n30:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\\n31:     return;\\n32:   }\\n33: \\n34:   // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\\n35:   // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\\n36:   // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\\n37:   // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\\n38:   //\\n39:   // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\\n40:   // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\\n41:   // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\\n42:   // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\\n43:   // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\\n44:   if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\\n45:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\\n46:   }\\n47: }\\n48: \\n49: void finalizeHeaders(FilterManagerCallbacks& callbacks, StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\\n50:                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n51:   const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route = stream_info.route();\\n52:   if (route != nullptr && route->routeEntry() != nullptr) {\\n53:     const Formatter::Context formatter_context{\\n54:         callbacks.requestHeaders().ptr(), &headers, {}, {}, {}, &callbacks.activeSpan()};\\n55:     route->routeEntry()->finalizeResponseHeaders(headers, formatter_context, stream_info);\\n56:   }\\n57: }\\n58: \\n59: } // namespace\\n60: \\n61: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {\\n62:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n63:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n64:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n65:     return;\\n66:   }\\n67: \\n68:   // Set ScopeTrackerScopeState if there's no existing crash context.\\n69:   ScopeTrackedObjectStack encapsulated_object;\\n70:   absl::optional<ScopeTrackerScopeState> state;\\n71:   if (parent_.dispatcher_.trackedObjectStackIsEmpty()) {\\n72:     restoreContextOnContinue(encapsulated_object);\\n73:     state.emplace(&encapsulated_object, parent_.dispatcher_);\\n74:   }\\n75: \\n76:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"continuing filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n77:                    static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n78:   ASSERT(!canIterate(),\\n79:          \\\"Attempting to continue iteration while the IterationState is already Continue\\\");\\n80:   // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, set iterate_from_current_filter_ to true so the\\n81:   // filter iteration starts with the current filter instead of the next one.\\n82:   if (stoppedAll()) {\\n83:     iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\\n84:   }\\n85:   allowIteration();\\n86: \\n87:   // Only resume with do1xxHeaders() if we've actually seen 1xx headers.\\n88:   if (has1xxHeaders()) {\\n89:     continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\\n90:     do1xxHeaders();\\n91:     // If the response headers have not yet come in, don't continue on with\\n92:     // headers and body. doHeaders expects request headers to exist.\\n93:     if (!parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders()) {\\n94:       return;\\n95:     }\\n96:   }\\n97: \\n98:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n99:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n100:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n101:     return;\\n102:   }\\n103: \\n104:   // Make sure that we handle the zero byte data frame case. We make no effort to optimize this\\n105:   // case in terms of merging it into a header only request/response. This could be done in the\\n106:   // future.\\n107:   if (!headers_continued_) {\\n108:     headers_continued_ = true;\\n109:     doHeaders(observedEndStream() && !bufferedData() && !hasTrailers());\\n110:   }\\n111: \\n112:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n113:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n114:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n115:     return;\\n116:   }\\n117: \\n118:   doMetadata();\\n119: \\n120:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n121:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n122:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n123:     return;\\n124:   }\\n125: \\n126:   // It is possible for trailers to be added during doData(). doData() itself handles continuation\\n127:   // of trailers for the non-continuation case. Thus, we must keep track of whether we had\\n128:   // trailers prior to calling doData(). If we do, then we continue them here, otherwise we rely\\n129:   // on doData() to do so.\\n130:   const bool had_trailers_before_data = hasTrailers();\\n131:   if (bufferedData()) {\\n132:     doData(observedEndStream() && !had_trailers_before_data);\\n133:   }\\n134: \\n135:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n136:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n137:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n138:     return;\\n139:   }\\n140: \\n141:   if (had_trailers_before_data) {\\n142:     doTrailers();\\n143:   }\\n144: \\n145:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = false;\\n146: }\\n147: \\n148: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status) {\\n149: IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\\n159:     return false;\\n160:   }\\n161: \\n162:   PANIC_DUE_TO_CORRUPT_ENUM;\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status,\\n166:                                                               bool& end_stream) {\\n167: IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\\n173:     break;\\n174:   case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer:\\n175:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllBuffer;\\n176: available yet)\\\", parent_);\\n184:     break;\\n185:   case FilterHeadersStatus::Continue:\\n186:     headers_continued_ = true;\\n187:     break;\\n188:   }\\n189: \\n190:     "content": "1: #include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n2: \n3: #include <functional>\n4: \n5: #include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n6: #include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n7: \n8: #include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n9: #include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n10: #include \"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n11: #include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n12: #include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n13: #include \"source/common/http/header_utility.h\"\n14: #include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n15: #include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n16: \n17: #include \"matching/data_impl.h\"\n18: \n19: namespace Envoy {\n20: namespace Http {\n21: \n22: namespace {\n23: \n24: // Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\n25: template <class Filters>\n26: void recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\n27:                             typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\n28:   // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\n29:   if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\n30:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n31:     return;\n32:   }\n33: \n34:   // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\n35:   // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\n36:   // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\n37:   // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\n38:   //\n39:   // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\n40:   // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\n41:   // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\n42:   // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\n43:   // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\n44:   if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\n45:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n46:   }\n47: }\n48: \n49: void finalizeHeaders(FilterManagerCallbacks& callbacks, StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\n50:                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n51:   const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route = stream_info.route();\n52:   if (route != nullptr && route->routeEntry() != nullptr) {\n53:     const Formatter::Context formatter_context{\n54:         callbacks.requestHeaders().ptr(), &headers, {}, {}, {}, &callbacks.activeSpan()};\n55:     route->routeEntry()->finalizeResponseHeaders(headers, formatter_context, stream_info);\n56:   }\n57: }\n58: \n59: } // namespace\n60: \n61: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {\n62:   if (!canContinue()) {\n63:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n64:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n65:     return;\n66:   }\n67: \n68:   // Set ScopeTrackerScopeState if there's no existing crash context.\n69:   ScopeTrackedObjectStack encapsulated_object;\n70:   absl::optional<ScopeTrackerScopeState> state;\n71:   if (parent_.dispatcher_.trackedObjectStackIsEmpty()) {\n72:     restoreContextOnContinue(encapsulated_object);\n73:     state.emplace(&encapsulated_object, parent_.dispatcher_);\n74:   }\n75: \n76:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"continuing filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n77:                    static_cast<const void*>(this));\n78:   ASSERT(!canIterate(),\n79:          \"Attempting to continue iteration while the IterationState is already Continue\");\n80:   // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, set iterate_from_current_filter_ to true so the\n81:   // filter iteration starts with the current filter instead of the next one.\n82:   if (stoppedAll()) {\n83:     iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\n84:   }\n85:   allowIteration();\n86: \n87:   // Only resume with do1xxHeaders() if we've actually seen 1xx headers.\n88:   if (has1xxHeaders()) {\n89:     continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n90:     do1xxHeaders();\n91:     // If the response headers have not yet come in, don't continue on with\n92:     // headers and body. doHeaders expects request headers to exist.\n93:     if (!parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders()) {\n94:       return;\n95:     }\n96:   }\n97: \n98:   if (!canContinue()) {\n99:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n100:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n101:     return;\n102:   }\n103: \n104:   // Make sure that we handle the zero byte data frame case. We make no effort to optimize this\n105:   // case in terms of merging it into a header only request/response. This could be done in the\n106:   // future.\n107:   if (!headers_continued_) {\n108:     headers_continued_ = true;\n109:     doHeaders(observedEndStream() && !bufferedData() && !hasTrailers());\n110:   }\n111: \n112:   if (!canContinue()) {\n113:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n114:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n115:     return;\n116:   }\n117: \n118:   doMetadata();\n119: \n120:   if (!canContinue()) {\n121:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n122:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n123:     return;\n124:   }\n125: \n126:   // It is possible for trailers to be added during doData(). doData() itself handles continuation\n127:   // of trailers for the non-continuation case. Thus, we must keep track of whether we had\n128:   // trailers prior to calling doData(). If we do, then we continue them here, otherwise we rely\n129:   // on doData() to do so.\n130:   const bool had_trailers_before_data = hasTrailers();\n131:   if (bufferedData()) {\n132:     doData(observedEndStream() && !had_trailers_before_data);\n133:   }\n134: \n135:   if (!canContinue()) {\n136:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n137:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n138:     return;\n139:   }\n140: \n141:   if (had_trailers_before_data) {\n142:     doTrailers();\n143:   }\n144: \n145:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = false;\n146: }\n147: \n148: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status) {\n149: false;\n160:   }\n161: \n162:   PANIC_DUE_TO_CORRUPT_ENUM;\n163: }\n164: \n165: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status,\n166: FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n172:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n173:     break;\n174:   case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer:\n175:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllBuffer;\n176:     break;\n177:   case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark:\n178:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n179: body (body not available yet)\", parent_);\n184:     break;\n185:   case FilterHeadersStatus::Continue:\n186:     headers_continued_ = true;\n187:     break;\n188:   }\n189: \n190:   handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback();\n191: \n192:   if (stoppedAll() || status == FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration) {\n193:     return false;\n194:   } else {\n195:     return true;\n196:   }\n197: }\n198: \n199: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleBufferData(Buffer::Instance& provided_data) {\n200: \n201: ",

19. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 500,
  "path": "source/common/http/filter_manager.cc",
  "repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy",
  "startLine": 200
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"200: \\n201:   // The way we do buffering is a little complicated which is why we have this common function\\n202:   // which is used for both encoding and decoding. When data first comes into our filter pipeline,\\n203:   // we send it through. Any filter can choose to stop iteration and buffer or not. If we then\\n204:   // continue iteration in the future, we use the buffered data. A future filter can stop and\\n205:   // buffer again. In this case, since we are already operating on buffered data, we don't\\n206:   // rebuffer, because we assume the filter has modified the buffer as it wishes in place.\\n207:   if (bufferedData().get() != &provided_data) {\\n208:     if (!bufferedData()) {\\n209:       bufferedData() = createBuffer();\\n210:     }\\n211:     bufferedData()->move(provided_data);\\n212:   }\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterDataCallback(FilterDataStatus status,\\n216:                                                            Buffer::Instance& provided_data,\\n217:                                                            bool& buffer_was_streaming) {\\n218: \\n219:   if (status == FilterDataStatus::Continue) {\\n220:     if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\\n221:       commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\\n222:       commonContinue();\\n223:       return false;\\n224:     } else {\\n225:       ASSERT(headers_continued_);\\n226:     }\\n227:   } else {\\n228:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\\n229:     if (status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer ||\\n230:         status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark) {\\n231:       buffer_was_streaming = status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark;\\n232:       commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\\n233:     } else if (observedEndStream() && !hasTrailers() && !bufferedData() &&\\n234:                // If the stream is destroyed, no need to handle the data buffer or trailers.\\n235:                // This can occur if the filter calls sendLocalReply.\\n236:                !parent_.state_.destroyed_) {\\n237:       // If this filter is doing StopIterationNoBuffer and this stream is terminated with a zero\\n238:       // byte data frame, we need to create an empty buffer to make sure that when commonContinue\\n239:       // is called, the pipeline resumes with an empty data frame with end_stream = true\\n240:       ASSERT(end_stream_);\\n241:       bufferedData() = createBuffer();\\n242:     }\\n243: \\n244:     return false;\\n245:   }\\n246: \\n247:   return true;\\n248: }\\n249: \\n250: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback(FilterTrailersStatus status) {\\n251: \\n252:   if (status == FilterTrailersStatus::Continue) {\\n253:     if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\\n254:       commonContinue();\\n255:       return false;\\n256:     } else {\\n257:       ASSERT(headers_continued_);\\n258:     }\\n259:   } else if (status == FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration) {\\n260:     if (canIterate()) {\\n261:       iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\\n262:     }\\n263:     return false;\\n264:   }\\n265: \\n266:   return true;\\n267: }\\n268: \\n269: OptRef<const Network::Connection> ActiveStreamFilterBase::connection() {\\n270:   return parent_.connection();\\n271: }\\n272: \\n273: Event::Dispatcher& ActiveStreamFilterBase::dispatcher() { return parent_.dispatcher_; }\\n274: \\n275: StreamInfo::StreamInfo& ActiveStreamFilterBase::streamInfo() { return parent_.streamInfo(); }\\n276: \\n277: Tracing::Span& ActiveStreamFilterBase::activeSpan() {\\n278:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.activeSpan();\\n279: }\\n280: \\n281: const ScopeTrackedObject& ActiveStreamFilterBase::scope() {\\n282:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.scope();\\n283: }\\n284: \\n285: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::restoreContextOnContinue(\\n286:     ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) {\\n287:   parent_.contextOnContinue(tracked_object_stack);\\n288: }\\n289: \\n290: OptRef<const Tracing::Config> ActiveStreamFilterBase::tracingConfig() const {\\n291:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.tracingConfig();\\n292: }\\n293: \\n294: Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr ActiveStreamFilterBase::clusterInfo() {\\n295:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.clusterInfo();\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: Router::RouteConstSharedPtr ActiveStreamFilterBase::route() { return getRoute(); }\\n299: \\n300: Router::RouteConstSharedPtr ActiveStreamFilterBase::getRoute() const {\\n301:   if (parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks()) {\\n302:     return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks()->route(nullptr);\\n303:   }\\n304:   return parent_.streamInfo().route();\\n305: }\\n306: \\n307: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::resetIdleTimer() {\\n308:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\\n309: }\\n310: \\n311: const Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig*\\n312: ActiveStreamFilterBase::mostSpecificPerFilterConfig() const {\\n313:   auto current_route = getRoute();\\n314:   if (current_route == nullptr) {\\n315:     return nullptr;\\n316:   }\\n317:   return current_route->mostSpecificPerFilterConfig(filter_context_.config_name);\\n318: }\\n319: \\n320: Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigs ActiveStreamFilterBase::perFilterConfigs() const {\\n321:   Router::RouteConstSharedPtr current_route = getRoute();\\n322:   if (current_route == nullptr) {\\n323:     return {};\\n324:   }\\n325: \\n326:   return current_route->perFilterConfigs(filter_context_.config_name);\\n327: }\\n328: \\n329: Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::http1StreamEncoderOptions() {\\n330:   // TODO(mattklein123): At some point we might want to actually wrap this interface but for now\\n331:   // we give the filter direct access to the encoder options.\\n332:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.http1StreamEncoderOptions();\\n333: }\\n334: \\n335: OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> ActiveStreamFilterBase::downstreamCallbacks() {\\n336:   return
