    "content": "900:     }\n901:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeMetadata;\n902:     FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeMetadata(metadata_map);\n903:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeMetadata;\n904: \n905:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode metadata called: filter={} status={}, metadata: {}\", *this,\n906:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\n907:                      metadata_map);\n908:     if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n909:       // If the decoder filter chain has been aborted, then either:\n910:       // 1. This filter has sent a local reply or GoAway from decode metadata.\n911:       // 2. This filter is the terminal http filter, and an upstream HTTP filter has sent a local\n912:       // reply.\n913:       ASSERT((status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) ||\n914:              (std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end()));\n915:       executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n916:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n917:                        \"decodeMetadata filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n918:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n919:       return;\n920:     }\n921: \n922:     // Add the processed metadata to the next entry.\n923:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\n924:       if (std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n925:         (*std::next(entry))\n926:             ->getSavedRequestMetadata()\n927:             ->emplace_back(std::make_unique<MetadataMap>(metadata_map));\n928:       }\n929:       (*entry)->commonContinue();\n930:       return;\n931:     }\n932:   }\n933: }\n934: \n935: void FilterManager::disarmRequestTimeout() { filter_manager_callbacks_.disarmRequestTimeout(); }\n936: \n937: StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator\n938: FilterManager::commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,\n939:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n940:   // Only do base state setting on the initial call. Subsequent calls for filtering do not touch\n941:   // the base state.\n942:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"commonEncodePrefix end_stream: {}, isHalfCloseEnabled: {}\", *this,\n943:                    end_stream, filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\n944:   if (filter == nullptr) {\n945:     if (end_stream) {\n946:       ASSERT(!state_.observed_encode_end_stream_);\n947:       state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\n948: \n949:       // When half close semantics are disabled, receiving end stream from the upstream causes\n950:       // decoder filter to stop, as neither filters nor upstream is interested in downstream data.\n951:       // half close is enabled in case tcp proxying is done with http1 encoder. In this case, we\n952:       // should not stop decoder filter chain when end_stream is true, as it will cause any data\n953:       // sent in the upstream direction to be\n954:       // dropped.\n955:       if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n956:         state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n957:       }\n958:     }\n959:     return encoder_filters_.begin();\n960:   }\n961: \n962:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n963:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n964:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n965:     // The current filter's encoding callback has not be called. Call it now.\n966:     return filter->entry();\n967:   }\n968:   return std::next(filter->entry());\n969: }\n970: \n971: StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator\n972: FilterManager::commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,\n973:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n974:   if (!filter) {\n975:     return decoder_filters_.begin();\n976:   }\n977:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n978:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n979:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n980:     // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\n981:     return filter->entry();\n982:   }\n983:   return std::next(filter->entry());\n984: }\n985: \n986: void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\n987:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\n988:   filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\n989: \n990:   for (auto entry : filters_) {\n991:     if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\n992:       data.reset_imminent_ = true;\n993:     }\n994:   }\n995:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\n996: }\n997: \n998: void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n999:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\n1000:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n1001:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n1002:   ASSERT(!state_.under_on_local_reply_);\n1003:   const bool is_head_request = state_.is_head_request_;\n1004:   const bool is_grpc_request = state_.is_grpc_request_;\n1005: \n1006:   // Stop filter chain iteration if local reply was sent while filter decoding or encoding callbacks\n1007:   // are running.\n1008:   if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n1009:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1010:   } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\n1011:     state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1012:   }\n1013:   // When independent half-close is enabled local reply always stops decoder filter chain.\n1014:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n1015:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1016:   }\n1017: \n1018:   streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n1019:   StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\n1020:   onLocalReply(data);\n1021:   if (data.reset_imminent_) {\n1022:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n1023:                      details);\n1024:     filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n1025:     return;\n1026:   }\n1027: \n1028:   if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n1029:       (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n1030:        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask))) {\n1031:     // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n1032:     // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n1033: \n1034:     if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n1035:       // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n1036:       // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n1037:       // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n1038:       cb->route(nullptr);\n1039:     }\n1040:     // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively invoking filters to avoid\n1041:     // re-entrant in filters.\n1042:     //\n1043:     // For reverse connections (where upstream initiates the connection to downstream), we need to\n1044:     // send local replies immediately rather than queuing them. This ensures proper handling of the\n1045:     // reversed connection flow and prevents potential issues with connection state and filter chain\n1046:     // processing.\n1047:     if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n1048:             \"envoy.reloadable_features.reverse_conn_force_local_reply\") &&\n1049:         (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask)) {\n1050:       prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n1051:                                       grpc_status, details);\n1052:     } else {\n1053:       sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n1054:                                    grpc_status, details);\n1055:     }\n1056:   } else if (!state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_) {\n1057:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Sending local reply with details {} directly to the encoder\", *this,\n1058:                      details);\n1059:     // In this case, at least the header and possibly the body has started\n1060:     // processing through the filter chain, but no non-informational headers\n1061:     // have been sent downstream. To ensure that filters don't get their\n1062:     // state machine screwed up, bypass the filter chain and send the local\n1063:     // reply directly to the codec.\n1064:     //\n1065:     sendDirectLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, state_.is_head_request_, grpc_status);\n1066:   } else {\n1067:     // If we land in this branch, response headers have already been sent to the client.\n1068:     // All we can do at this point is reset the stream.\n1069:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. Prior headers have already been sent\",\n1070:                      *this, details);\n1071:     // TODO(snowp): This means we increment the tx_reset stat which we weren't doing previously.\n1072:     // Intended?\n1073:     filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream();\n1074:   }\n1075: }\n1076: \n1077: void DownstreamFilterManager::prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(\n1078:     bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,\n1079:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,\n1080:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n1081:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Preparing local reply with details {}\", *this, details);\n1082:   ASSERT(!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value());\n1083:   // For early error handling, do a best-effort attempt to create a filter chain\n1084:   // to ensure access logging. If the filter chain already exists this will be\n1085:   // a no-op.\n1086:   createDownstreamFilterChain();\n1087: \n1088:   if (prepared_local_reply_) {\n1089:     return;\n1090:   }\n1091: \n1092:   prepared_local_reply_ = Utility::prepareLocalReply(\n1093:       Utility::EncodeFunctions{\n1094:           [this, modify_headers](ResponseHeaderMap& headers) -> void {\n1095:             finalizeHeaders(filter_manager_callbacks_, streamInfo(), headers);\n1096:             if (modify_headers) {\n1097:               modify_headers(headers);\n1098:             }\n1099:           },\n1100:           [this](ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, Code& code, std::string& body,\n1101:                  absl::string_view& content_type) -> void {\n1102:             local_reply_.rewrite(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders().ptr(), response_headers,\n1103:                                  streamInfo(), code, body, content_type);\n1104:           },\n1105:           [this](ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) -> void {\n1106:             filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseHeaders(std::move(headers));\n1107:             encodeHeaders(nullptr, filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().ref(), end_stream);\n1108:           },\n1109:           [this](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) -> void {\n1110:             encodeData(nullptr, data, end_stream,\n1111:                        FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n1112:           }},\n1113:       Utility::LocalReplyData{is_grpc_request, code, body, grpc_status, is_head_request});\n1114: }\n1115: \n1116: void DownstreamFilterManager::executeLocalReplyIfPrepared() {\n1117:   if (!prepared_local_reply_) {\n1118:     return;\n1119:   }\n1120: \n1121:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Executing sending local reply.\", *this);\n1122:   Utility::encodeLocalReply(state_.destroyed_, std::move(prepared_local_reply_));\n1123: }\n1124: \n1125: FilterManager::CreateChainResult DownstreamFilterManager::createDownstreamFilterChain() {\n1126:   return createFilterChain(filter_chain_factory_);\n1127: }\n1128: \n1129: void   "content": "{\"content\":\"1200:             if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\\n1201:               return;\\n1202:             }\\n1203:             maybeEndEncode(end_stream);\\n1204:           }},\\n1205:       Utility::LocalReplyData{state_.is_grpc_request_, code, body, grpc_status, is_head_request});\\n1206: }\\n1207: \\n1208: void FilterManager::encode1xxHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\\n1209:                                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n1210:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\\n1211:   ASSERT(proxy_100_continue_);\\n1212:   // The caller must guarantee that encode1xxHeaders() is invoked at most once.\\n1213:   ASSERT(!state_.has_1xx_headers_ || filter != nullptr);\\n1214:   // Make sure commonContinue continues encode1xxHeaders.\\n1215:   state_.has_1xx_headers_ = true;\\n1216: \\n1217:   // Similar to the block in encodeHeaders, run encode1xxHeaders on each\\n1218:   // filter. This is simpler than that case because 100 continue implies no\\n1219:   // end-stream, and because there are normal headers coming there's no need for\\n1220:   // complex continuation logic.\\n1221:   // 100-continue filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\\n1222:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\\n1223:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\\n1224:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1225:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n1226:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders));\\n1227:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\\n1228:     const Filter1xxHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encode1xxHeaders(headers);\\n1229:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\\n1230: \\n1231:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"encode 1xx continue headers called: filter={} status={}\\\", *this,\\n1232:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\\n1233:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n1234:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n1235:                        \\\"encode1xxHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n1236:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n1237:       return;\\n1238:     }\\n1239: \\n1240:     if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(status)) {\\n1241:       return;\\n1242:     }\\n1243:   }\\n1244: \\n1245:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encode1xxHeaders(headers);\\n1246: }\\n1247: \\n1248: void FilterManager::maybeContinueEncoding(StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry) {\\n1249:   if (continue_data_entry != encoder_filters_.end()) {\\n1250:     // We use the continueEncoding() code since it will correctly handle not calling\\n1251:     // encodeHeaders() again. Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueEncoding() code\\n1252:     // expects it.\\n1253:     ASSERT(buffered_response_data_);\\n1254:     (*continue_data_entry)->iteration_state_ =\\n1255:         ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\\n1256:     (*continue_data_entry)->continueEncoding();\\n1257:   }\\n1258: }\\n1259: \\n1260: void FilterManager::encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\\n1261:                                   bool end_stream) {\\n1262:   // See encodeHeaders() comments in envoy/http/filter.h for why the 1xx precondition holds.\\n1263:   ASSERT(!CodeUtility::is1xx(Utility::getResponseStatus(headers)) ||\\n1264:          Utility::getResponseStatus(headers) == enumToInt(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols));\\n1265:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\\n1266:   disarmRequestTimeout();\\n1267: \\n1268:   // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\\n1269:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\\n1270:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, end_stream, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\\n1271:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = encoder_filters_.end();\\n1272: \\n1273:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1274:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n1275:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders));\\n1276:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\\n1277:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\\n1278:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1279:       state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\\n1280:     }\\n1281:     FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\\n1282:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n1283:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n1284:                        \\\"encodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n1285:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n1286:       status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n1287:     }\\n1288: \\n1289:     ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\\n1290:            \\\"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \\\"\\n1291:            \\\"encodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\\\");\\n1292: \\n1293:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\\n1294:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1295:       state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\\n1296:     }\\n1297:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"encode headers called: filter={} status={}\\\", *this,\\n1298:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\\n1299: \\n1300:     (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\\n1301: \\n1302:     const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\\n1303: \\n1304:     // If this filter ended the stream, encodeComplete() should be called for it.\\n1305:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1306:       (*entry)->handle_->encodeComplete();\\n1307:     }\\n1308: \\n1309:     if (!continue_iteration) {\\n1310:       if (!(*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1311:         maybeContinueEncoding(continue_data_entry);\\n1312:       }\\n1313:       return;\\n1314:     }\\n1315: \\n1316:     // Here we handle the case where we have a header only response, but a filter adds a body\\n1317:     // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\\n1318:     if (end_stream && buffered_response_data_ && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\\n1319:       continue_data_entry = entry;\\n1320:     }\\n1321:   }\\n1322: \\n1323:   // Check if the filter chain above did not remove critical headers or set malformed header values.\\n1324:   // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\\n1325:   // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\\n1326:   // See the previous attempt (#15658) for detail, and for now we choose to protect only against\\n1327:   // filter chains.\\n1328:   const auto status = HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(headers);\\n1329:   if (!status.ok()) {\\n1330:     // If the check failed, then we reply with BadGateway, and stop the further processing.\\n1331:     sendLocalReply(\\n1332:         Http::Code::BadGateway, status.message(), nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n1333:         absl::StrCat(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterRemovedRequiredResponseHeaders,\\n1334:                      \\\"{\\\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(status.message()), \\\"}\\\"));\\n1335:     return;\\n1336:   }\\n1337: \\n1338:   const bool modified_end_stream = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\\n1339:   state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_ = true;\\n1340:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders(headers, modified_end_stream);\\n1341:   if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\\n1342:     return;\\n1343:   }\\n1344:   maybeEndEncode(modified_end_stream);\\n1345: \\n1346:   if (!modified_end_stream) {\\n1347:     maybeContinueEncoding(continue_data_entry);\\n1348:   }\\n1349: }\\n1350: \\n1351: void FilterManager::encodeMetadata(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\\n1352:                                    MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\\n1353:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\\n1354: \\n1355:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\\n1356:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\\n1357: \\n1358:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1359:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n1360:     // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, stores metadata and returns.\\n1361:     // If the filter pointed by entry hasn't returned from encodeHeaders, stores newly added\\n1362:     // metadata in case encodeHeaders returns StopAllIteration. The latter can happen when headers\\n1363:     // callbacks generate new metadata.\\n1364:     if (!(*entry)->processed_headers_ || (*entry)->stoppedAll()) {\\n1365:       (*entry)->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\\n1366:       return;\\n1367:     }\\n1368: \\n1369:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\\n1370: \\n1371:     FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeMetadata(*metadata_map_ptr);\\n1372: \\n1373:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\\n1374: \\n1375:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"encode metadata called: filter={} status={} metadata={}\\\", *this,\\n1376:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\\n1377:                      *metadata_map_ptr);\\n1378: \\n1379:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) {\\n1380:       // In case of a local reply, we do not continue encoding metadata. Metadata is not sent on to\\n1381:       // the client.\\n1382:       return;\\n1383:     }\\n1384: \\n1385:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\\n1386:       if (std::next(entry) != encoder_filters_.end()) {\\n1387:         (*std::next(entry))->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\\n1388:       } else {\\n1389:         filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\\n1390:       }\\n1391:       (*entry)->commonContinue();\\n1392:       return;\\n1393:     }\\n1394:   }\\n1395:   // TODO(soya3129): update stats with metadata.\\n1396: \\n1397:   // Now encode metadata via the codec.\\n1398:   if (!metadata_map_ptr->empty()) {\\n1399:     filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\\n1400:   }\\n1401: }\\n1402: \\n1403: ResponseTrailerMap& FilterManager::addEncodedTrailers() {\\n1404:   // Trailers can only be added during the last data frame (i.e. end_stream = true).\\n1405:   ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EndOfStream);\\n1406: \\n1407:   // Trailers can only be added once.\\n1408:   ASSERT(!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers());\\n1409: \\n1410:   filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapImpl::create());\\n1411:   return *filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\\n1412: }\\n1413: \\n1414: void FilterManager::addEncodedData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\\n1415:                                    bool streaming) {\\n1416:   if (state_.filter_call_state_ == 0 ||\\n1417:       (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders) ||\\n1418:       (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeData) ||\\n1419:       ((state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeTrailers) && !filter.canIterate())) {\\n1420:     // Make sure if this triggers watermarks, the correct action is taken.\\n1421:     state_.encoder_filters_streaming_ = streaming;\\n1422:     // If no call is happening or we are in the decode headers/data callback, buffer the data.\\n1423:     // Inline processing happens in the decodeHeaders() callback if necessary.\\n1424:     filter.commonHandleBufferData(data);\\n1425:   } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeTrailers) {\\n1426:     // In this case we need to inline dispatch the data to further filters. If those filters\\n1427:     // choose to buffer/stop iteration that's fine.\\n1428:     encodeData(&filter, data, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\\n1429:   } else {\\n1430:     IS_ENVOY_BUG(\\\"Invalid response data\\\");\\n1431:     sendLocalReply(Http::Code::BadGateway, \\\"Filter error\\\", nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n1432:                    StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterAddedInvalidResponseData);\\n1433:   }\\n1434: }\\n1435: \\n1436: void FilterManager::encodeData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\\n1437:                                bool end_stream,\\n1438:                                FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\\n1439:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\\n1440: \\n1441:   // Filter iteration may start at the current filter.\\n1442:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\\n1443:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, end_stream, filter_iteration_start_state);\\n1444:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator trailers_added_entry = encoder_filters_.end();\\n1445: \\n1446:   const bool trailers_exists_at_start = filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers().has_value();\\n1447:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1448:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n1449:     // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, return now.\\n1450:     if (handleDataIfStopAll(**entry, data, state_.encoder_filters_streaming_)) {\\n1451:       return;\\n1452:     }\\n1453:     // If end_stream_ is marked for a filter, the data is not for this filter and filters after.\\n1454:     // For details, please see the comment in the ActiveStream::decodeData() function.\\n1455:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1456:       return;\\n1457:     }\\n1458:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeData));\\n1459: \\n1460:     // We check the response_trailers_ pointer here in case addEncodedTrailers\\n1461:     // is called in encodeData during a previous filter invocation, at which point we communicate to\\n1462:     // the current and future filters that the stream has not yet ended.\\n1463:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeData;\\n1464:     if (end_stream) {\\n1465:       state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\\n1466:     }\\n1467: \\n1468:     recordLatestDataFilter(entry, state_.latest_data_encoding_filter_, encoder_filters_);\\n1469: \\n1470:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = end_stream && !filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\\n1471:     FilterDataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeData(data, (*entry)->end_stream_);\\n1472:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n1473:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"encodeData filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n1474:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n1475:       status = FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\\n1476:     }\\n1477:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1478:       (*entry)->handle_->encodeComplete();\\n1479:     }\\n1480:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeData;\\n1481:     if (end_stream) {\\n1482:       state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\\n1483:     }\\n1484:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"encode data called: filter={} status={}\\\", *this,\\n1485:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\\n1486: \\n1487:     if (!trailers_exists_at_start && filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers() &&\\n1488:         trailers_added_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\\n1489:       trailers_added_entry = entry;\\n1490:     }\\n1491: \\n1492:     if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfterDataCallback(status, data, state_.encoder_filters_streaming_)) {\\n1493:       return;\\n1494:     }\\n1495:   }\\n1496: \\n1497:   const bool modified_end_stream = end_stream && trailers_added_entry == encoder_filters_.end();\\n1498:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeData(data, modified_end_stream);\\n1499:   if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\\n1500:     return;\\n1501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1200-1500\"}",

    "content": "1200:             if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\n1201:               return;\n1202:             }\n1203:             maybeEndEncode(end_stream);\n1204:           }},\n1205:       Utility::LocalReplyData{state_.is_grpc_request_, code, body, grpc_status, is_head_request});\n1206: }\n1207: \n1208: void FilterManager::encode1xxHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\n1209:                                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n1210:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n1211:   ASSERT(proxy_100_continue_);\n1212:   // The caller must guarantee that encode1xxHeaders() is invoked at most once.\n1213:   ASSERT(!state_.has_1xx_headers_ || filter != nullptr);\n1214:   // Make sure commonContinue continues encode1xxHeaders.\n1215:   state_.has_1xx_headers_ = true;\n1216: \n1217:   // Similar to the block in encodeHeaders, run encode1xxHeaders on each\n1218:   // filter. This is simpler than that case because 100 continue implies no\n1219:   // end-stream, and because there are normal headers coming there's no need for\n1220:   // complex continuation logic.\n1221:   // 100-continue filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n1222:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n1223:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n1224:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n1225:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n1226:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders));\n1227:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\n1228:     const Filter1xxHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encode1xxHeaders(headers);\n1229:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\n1230: \n1231:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode 1xx continue headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n1232:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n1233:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n1234:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n1235:                        \"encode1xxHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n1236:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n1237:       return;\n1238:     }\n1239: \n1240:     if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(status)) {\n1241:       return;\n1242:     }\n1243:   }\n1244: \n1245:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encode1xxHeaders(headers);\n1246: }\n1247: \n1248: void FilterManager::maybeContinueEncoding(StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry) {\n1249:   if (continue_data_entry != encoder_filters_.end()) {\n1250:     // We use the continueEncoding() code since it will correctly handle not calling\n1251:     // encodeHeaders() again. Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueEncoding() code\n1252:     // expects it.\n1253:     ASSERT(buffered_response_data_);\n1254:     (*continue_data_entry)->iteration_state_ =\n1255:         ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n1256:     (*continue_data_entry)->continueEncoding();\n1257:   }\n1258: }\n1259: \n1260: void FilterManager::encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\n1261:                                   bool end_stream) {\n1262:   // See encodeHeaders() comments in envoy/http/filter.h for why the 1xx precondition holds.\n1263:   ASSERT(!CodeUtility::is1xx(Utility::getResponseStatus(headers)) ||\n1264:          Utility::getResponseStatus(headers) == enumToInt(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols));\n1265:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n1266:   disarmRequestTimeout();\n1267: \n1268:   // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n1269:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n1270:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, end_stream, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n1271:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = encoder_filters_.end();\n1272: \n1273:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n1274:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n1275:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders));\n1276:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\n1277:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\n1278:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n1279:       state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n1280:     }\n1281:     FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n1282:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n1283:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n1284:                        \"encodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n1285:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n1286:       status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n1287:     }\n1288: \n1289:     ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n1290:            \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n1291:            \"encodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\");\n1292: \n1293:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\n1294:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n1295:       state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n1296:     }\n1297:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n1298:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n1299: \n1300:     (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\n1301: \n1302:     const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n1303: \n1304:     // If this filter ended the stream, encodeComplete() should be called for it.\n1305:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n1306:       (*entry)->handle_->encodeComplete();\n1307:     }\n1308: \n1309:     if (!continue_iteration) {\n1310:       if (!(*entry)->end_stream_) {\n1311:         maybeContinueEncoding(continue_data_entry);\n1312:       }\n1313:       return;\n1314:     }\n1315: \n1316:     // Here we handle the case where we have a header only response, but a filter adds a body\n1317:     // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n1318:     if (end_stream && buffered_response_data_ && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\n1319:       continue_data_entry = entry;\n1320:     }\n1321:   }\n1322: \n1323:   // Check if the filter chain above did not remove critical headers or set malformed header values.\n1324:   // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\n1325:   // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\n1326:   // See the previous attempt (#15658) for detail, and for now we choose to protect only against\n1327:   // filter chains.\n1328:   const auto status = HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(headers);\n1329:   if (!status.ok()) {\n1330:     // If the check failed, then we reply with BadGateway, and stop the further processing.\n1331:     sendLocalReply(\n1332:         Http::Code::BadGateway, status.message(), nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n1333:         absl::StrCat(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterRemovedRequiredResponseHeaders,\n1334:                      \"{\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(status.message()), \"}\"));\n1335:     return;\n1336:   }\n1337: \n1338:   const bool modified_end_stream = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\n1339:   state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_ = true;\n1340:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders(headers, modified_end_stream);\n1341:   if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\n1342:     return;\n1343:   }\n1344:   maybeEndEncode(modified_end_stream);\n1345: \n1346:   if (!modified_end_stream) {\n1347:     maybeContinueEncoding(continue_data_entry);\n1348:   }\n1349: }\n1350: \n1351: void FilterManager::encodeMetadata(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\n1352:                                    MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\n1353:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n1354: \n1355:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n1356:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n1357: \n1358:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n1359:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n1360:     // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, stores metadata and returns.\n1361:     // If the filter pointed by entry hasn't returned from encodeHeaders, stores newly added\n1362:     // metadata in case encodeHeaders returns StopAllIteration. The latter can happen when headers\n1363:     // callbacks generate new metadata.\n1364:     if (!(*entry)->processed_headers_ || (*entry)->stoppedAll()) {\n1365:       (*entry)->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n1366:       return;\n1367:     }\n1368: \n1369:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\n1370: \n1371:     FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeMetadata(*metadata_map_ptr);\n1372: \n1373:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\n1374: \n1375:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode metadata called: filter={} status={} metadata={}\", *this,\n1376:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\n1377:                      *metadata_map_ptr);\n1378: \n1379:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) {\n1380:       // In case of a local reply, we do not continue encoding metadata. Metadata is not sent on to\n1381:       // the client.\n1382:       return;\n1383:     }\n1384: \n1385:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\n1386:       if (std::next(entry) != encoder_filters_.end()) {\n1387:         (*std::next(entry))->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n1388:       } else {\n1389:         filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n1390:       }\n1391:       (*entry)->commonContinue();\n1392:       return;\n1393:     }\n1394:   }\n1395:   // TODO(soya3129): update stats with metadata.\n1396: \n1397:   // Now encode metadata via the codec.\n1398:   if (!metadata_map_ptr->empty()) {\n1399:     filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n1400:   }\n1401: }\n1402: \n1403: ResponseTrailerMap& FilterManager::addEncodedTrailers() {\n1404:   // Trailers can only be added during the last data frame (i.e. end_stream = true).\n1405:   ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EndOfStream);\n1406: \n1407:   // Trailers can only be added once.\n1408:   ASSERT(!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers());\n1409: \n1410:   filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapImpl::create());\n1411:   return *filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\n1412: }\n1413: \n1414: void FilterManager::addEncodedData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n1415:                                    bool streaming) {\n1416:   if (state_.filter_call_state_ == 0 ||\n1417:       (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders) ||\n1418:       (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeData) ||\n1419:       ((state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeTrailers) && !filter.canIterate())) {\n1420:     // Make sure if this triggers watermarks, the correct action is taken.\n1421:     state_.encoder_filters_streaming_ = streaming;\n1422:     // If no call is happening or we are in the decode headers/data callback, buffer the data.\n1423:     // Inline processing happens in the decodeHeaders() callback if necessary.\n1424:     filter.commonHandleBufferData(data);\n1425:   } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeTrailers) {\n1426:     // In this case we need to inline dispatch the data to further filters. If those filters\n1427:     // choose to buffer/stop iteration that's fine.\n1428:     encodeData(&filter, data, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n1429:   } else {\n1430:     IS_ENVOY_BUG(\"Invalid response data\");\n1431:     sendLocalReply(Http::Code::BadGateway, \"Filter error\", nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n1432:                    StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterAddedInvalidResponseData);\n1433:   }\n1434: }\n1435: \n1436: void FilterManager::encodeData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n1437:                                bool end_stream,\n1438:                                FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n1439:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n1440: \n1441:   // Filter iteration may start at the current filter.\n1442:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n1443:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, end_stream, filter_iteration_start_state);\n1444:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator trailers_added_entry = encoder_filters_.end();\n1445: \n1446:   const bool trailers_exists_at_start = filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers().has_value();\n1447:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n1448:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n1449:     // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, return now.\n1450:     if (handleDataIfStopAll(**entry, data, state_.encoder_filters_streaming_)) {\n1451:       return;\n1452:     }\n1453:     // If end_stream_ is marked for a filter, the data is not for this filter and filters after.\n1454:     // For details, please see the comment in the ActiveStream::decodeData() function.\n1455:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n1456:       return;\n1457:     }\n1458:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeData));\n1459: \n1460:     // We check the response_trailers_ pointer here in case addEncodedTrailers\n1461:     // is called in encodeData during a previous filter invocation, at which point we communicate to\n1462:     // the current and future filters that the stream has not yet ended.\n1463:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeData;\n1464:     if encoder_filters_);\n1469: \n1470:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = end_stream && !filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\n1471:     FilterDataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeData(data, entry =\\n1517:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, true, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\\n1518:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1519:   state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ = true;\\n1548:     if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\\n1549:       // If independent half close is enabled the stream is closed when both decoder and encoder\\n1550:       // filter chains has completed or were aborted.\\n1551:       checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\\n1552:     } else {\\n1553:       // Otherwise encoding end_stream always closes the stream (and resets it if request was not\\n1554:       // complete yet).\\n1555:       filter_manager_callbacks_.endStream();\\n1556:     }\\n1557:   }\\n1558: }\\n1559: \\n1560: void FilterManager::maybeEndDecode(bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream) {\\n1561:   if (terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream) {\\n1562:     ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_);\\n1563:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ = true;\\n1564:     // If the decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. due to local reply)\\n1565:     // we rely on the encoding of end_stream to close the stream.\\n1566:     if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled() && !stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\\n1567:       checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\\n1568:     }\\n1569:   }\\n1570: }\\n1571: \\n1572: void FilterManager::checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed() {\\n1573:   ASSERT(filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\\n1574:   // When the independent half close is enabled the stream is always closed on error responses\\n1575:   // from the server.\\n1576:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value()) {\\n1577:     const uint64_t response_status =\\n1578:         Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().ref());\\n1579:     const bool error_response =\\n1580:         !(Http::CodeUtility::is2xx(response_status) || Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_status));\\n1581:     // Abort the decoder filter if it has not yet been completed.\\n1582:     if (error_response && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_) {\\n1583:       state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1584:     }\\n1585:   }\\n1586: \\n1587:   // Handle the case where the end_stream was received from both downstream client and upstream\\n1588:   // server but a decoder filter added either request body or trailers AND paused decoder filter\\n1589:   // chain iteration. This case is currently handled the same way as if independent half-close is\\n1590:   // disabled, i.e. proxying is stopped as soon as encoding has finished.\\n1591:   // TODO(yanavlasov): to support this case the codec needs to notify HCM that it has closed its low\\n1592:   // level stream\\n1593:   //                   to avoid use-after-free when HCM cleans-up its ActiveStream\\n1594:   const bool downstream_client_sent_end_stream = decoderObservedEndStream();\\n1595:   const bool decoder_filter_chain_paused =\\n1596:       !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_;\\n1597:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && downstream_client_sent_end_stream &&\\n1598:       decoder_filter_chain_paused) {\\n1599:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1600:   }\\n1601: \\n1602:   // If independent half close is enabled then close the stream when\\n1603:   // 1. Both encoder and decoder filter chains has completed.\\n1604:   // 2. Encoder filter chain has completed and decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. local reply).\\n1605:   // There is no need to check for aborted encoder filter chain as the filter will either be\\n1606:   // completed or stream is reset.\\n1607:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\\n1608:       (state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_)) {\\n1609: FilterManager::handleDataIfStopAll(ActiveStreamFilterBase& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\\n1626:                                         bool& filter_streaming) {\\n1627:   if (filter.stoppedAll()) {\\n1628:     ASSERT(!filter.canIterate());\\n1629:     filter_streaming =\\n1630:         filter.iteration_state_ == ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\\n1631: FilterManager::createUpgradeFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory,\\n1672:                                         FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl& callbacks) {\\n1673:   const HeaderEntry* upgrade = nullptr;\\n1674:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()) {\\n1675:     upgrade = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()->Upgrade();\\n1676: \\n1677:     // Treat CONNECT requests as a special upgrade case.\\n1678:     if (!upgrade && HeaderUtility::isConnect(*filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders())) {\\n1679:       upgrade = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()->Method();\\n1680:     }\\n1681:   }\\n1682: \\n1683:   if (upgrade == nullptr) {\\n1684:     // No upgrade header, no upgrade filter chain.\\n1685:     return UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\\n1686:   }\\n1687: \\n1688:   const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgrade_map = filter_manager_callbacks_.upgradeMap();\\n1689:   return filter_chain_factory.createUpgradeFilterChain(upgrade->value().getStringView(),\\n1690:                                                        upgrade_map, callbacks)\\n1691:              ? UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted\\n1692:              : UpgradeResult::UpgradeRejected;\\n1693: }\\n1694: \\n1695: FilterManager::CreateChainResult\\n1696: FilterManager::createFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory) {\\n1697:   if (state_.create_chain_result_.created()) {\\n1698:     return state_.create_chain_result_;\\n1699:   }\\n1700: \\n1701:   // After the filter chain creation is completed, the filter chain containers will not be\\n1702:   // modified. So, we can safely complete the initialization of the filter chain now.\\n1703:   Cleanup initialize_filter_chain([this]() {\\n1704:     for (auto iter = decoder_filters_.begin(); iter != decoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\\n1705:       (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\\n1706:     }\\n1707:     for (auto iter = encoder_filters_.begin(); iter != encoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\\n1708:       (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\\n1709:     }\\n1710:   });\\n1711: \\n1712:   // TODO(wbpcode): reserve memory for filters to avoid frequent reallocation.\\n1713: \\n1714:   OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstream_callbacks =\\n1715:       filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks();\\n1716: \\n1717:   FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl callbacks(*this);\\n1718: \\n1719:   UpgradeResult upgrade = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\\n1720: \\n1721:   // Only try the upgrade filter chain for downstream filter chains.\\n1722:   if (downstream_callbacks.has_value()) {\\n1723:     upgrade = createUpgradeFilterChain(filter_chain_factory, callbacks);\\n1724:     if (upgrade == UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted) {\\n1725:       // Upgrade filter chain is created. Return the result directly.\\n1726:       state_.create_chain_result_ = CreateChainResult(true, upgrade);\\n1727:       return state_.create_chain_result_;\\n1728:     }\\n1729:     // If the upgrade is unnecessary or the upgrade filter chain is rejected, fall through to\\n1730:     // create the default filter chain.\\n1731:   }\\n1732: \\n1733:   state_.create_chain_result_ =\\n1734:       CreateChainResult(filter_chain_factory.createFilterChain(callbacks), upgrade);\\n1735:   return state_.create_chain_result_;\\n1736: }\\n1737: \\n1738: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::requestDataDrained() {\\n1739:   // If this is called it means the call to requestDataTooLarge() was a\\n1740:   // streaming call, or a 413 would have been sent.\\n1741:   onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\\n1742: }\\n1743: \\n1744: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() {\\n1745:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\\n1746: }\\n1747: \\n1748: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(\\n1749:     DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) {\\n1750:   // This is called exactly once per upstream-stream, by the router filter. Therefore, we\\n1751:   // expect the same callbacks to not be registered twice.\\n1752:   ASSERT(std::find(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.begin(), parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(),\\n1753:                    &watermark_callbacks) == parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end());\\n1754:   parent_.watermark_callbacks_.emplace(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(), &watermark_callbacks);\\n1755:   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < parent_.high_watermark_count_; ++i) {\\n1756:     watermark_callbacks.onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\\n1757:   }\\n1758: }\\n1759: \\n1760: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(\\n1761:     DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) {\\n1762:   ASSERT(std::find(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.begin(), parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(),\\n1763:                    &watermark_callbacks) != parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end());\\n1764:   parent_.watermark_callbacks_.remove(&watermark_callbacks);\\n1765: }\\n1766: \\n1767: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream(const ResponseHeaderMap* headers) {\\n1768:   // Because the filter's and the HCM view of if the stream has a body and if\\n1769:   // the stream is complete may differ, re-check bytesReceived() to make sure\\n1770:   // there was no body from the HCM's point of view.\\n1771:   if (!observedEndStream()) {\\n1772:     return false;\\n1773:   }\\n1774: \\n1775:   parent_.state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1776:   parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1777:   parent_.state_.recreated_stream_ = true;\\n1778: \\n1779:   parent_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\\n1780:       StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().InternalRedirect);\\n1781: \\n1782:   if (headers != nullptr) {\\n1783:     // The call to setResponseHeaders is needed to ensure that the headers are properly logged in\\n1784:     // access logs before the stream is destroyed. Since the function expects a\\n1785:     // ResponseHeaderPtr&&, ownership of the headers must be passed. This cannot happen earlier in\\n1786:     // the flow (such as in the call to setupRedirect) because at that point it is still possible\\n1787:     // for the headers to be used in a different logical branch. We work around this by creating a\\n1788:     // copy and passing ownership of the copy instead.\\n1789:     ResponseHeaderMapPtr headers_copy = createHeaderMap<ResponseHeaderMapImpl>(*headers);\\n1790:     parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseHeaders(std::move(headers_copy));\\n1791:     parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.chargeStats(*headers);\\n1792:   }\\n1793: \\n1794:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.recreateStream(parent_.streamInfo().filterState());\\n1795: \\n1796:   return true;\\n1797: }\\n1798: \\n1799: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addUpstreamSocketOptions(\\n1800:     const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options) {\\n1801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1500-1800\"}",

{\n1640:     watermark_callbacks->onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n1641:   }\n1642: }\n1643: \n1644: void FilterManager::callLowWatermarkCallbacks() {\n1645:   ASSERT(high_watermark_count_ > 0);\n1646:   --high_watermark_count_;\n1647:   for (auto watermark_callbacks : watermark_callbacks_) {\n1648:     watermark_callbacks->onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n1649:   }\n1650: }\n1651: \n1652: void FilterManager::setBufferLimit(uint64_t new_limit) {\n1653:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"setting buffer limit to {}\", *this, new_limit);\n1654:   buffer_limit_ = new_limit;\n1655:   if (buffered_request_data_) {\n1656:     buffered_request_data_->setWatermarks(buffer_limit_);\n1657:   }\n1658:   if (buffered_response_data_) {\n1659:     buffered_response_data_->setWatermarks(buffer_limit_);\n1660:   }\n1661: }\n1662: \n1663: void FilterManager::contextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) {\n1664:   if (connection_.has_value()) {\n1665:     tracked_object_stack.add(*connection_);\n1666:   }\n1667:   tracked_object_stack.add(filter_manager_callbacks_.scope());\n1668: }\n1669: \n1670: FilterManager::UpgradeResult\n1671: FilterManager::createUpgradeFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory,\n1672:                                         FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl& callbacks) {\n1673:   const HeaderEntry* upgrade = nullptr;\n1674:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()) {\n1675:     upgrade = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()->Upgrade();\n1676: \n1677:     // Treat CONNECT requests as a special upgrade case.\n1678:     if (!upgrade && HeaderUtility::isConnect(*filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders())) {\n1679:       upgrade = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders()->Method();\n1680:     }\n1681:   }\n1682: \n1683:   if (upgrade == nullptr) {\n1684:     // No upgrade header, no upgrade filter chain.\n1685:     return UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\n1686:   }\n1687: \n1688:   const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgrade_map = filter_manager_callbacks_.upgradeMap();\n1689:   return filter_chain_factory.createUpgradeFilterChain(upgrade->value().getStringView(),\n1690:                                                        upgrade_map, callbacks)\n1691:              ? UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted\n1692:              : UpgradeResult::UpgradeRejected;\n1693: }\n1694: \n1695: FilterManager::CreateChainResult\n1696: FilterManager::createFilterChain(const FilterChainFactory& filter_chain_factory) {\n1697:   if (state_.create_chain_result_.created()) {\n1698:     return state_.create_chain_result_;\n1699:   }\n1700: \n1701:   // After the filter chain creation is completed, the filter chain containers will not be\n1702:   // modified. So, we can safely complete the initialization of the filter chain now.\n1703:   Cleanup initialize_filter_chain([this]() {\n1704:     for (auto iter = decoder_filters_.begin(); iter != decoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\n1705:       (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\n1706:     }\n1707:     for (auto iter = encoder_filters_.begin(); iter != encoder_filters_.end(); ++iter) {\n1708:       (*iter)->entry_ = iter;\n1709:     }\n1710:   });\n1711: \n1712:   // TODO(wbpcode): reserve memory for filters to avoid frequent reallocation.\n1713: \n1714:   OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstream_callbacks =\n1715:       filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks();\n1716: \n1717:   FilterChainFactoryCallbacksImpl callbacks(*this);\n1718: \n1719:   UpgradeResult upgrade = UpgradeResult::UpgradeUnneeded;\n1720: \n1721:   // Only try the upgrade filter chain for downstream filter chains.\n1722:   if (downstream_callbacks.has_value()) {\n1723:     upgrade = createUpgradeFilterChain(filter_chain_factory, callbacks);\n1724:     if (upgrade == UpgradeResult::UpgradeAccepted) {\n1725:       // Upgrade filter chain is created. Return the result directly.\n1726:       state_.create_chain_result_ = CreateChainResult(true, upgrade);\n1727:       return state_.create_chain_result_;\n1728:     }\n1729:     // If the upgrade is unnecessary or the upgrade filter chain is rejected, fall through to\n1730:     // create the default filter chain.\n1731:   }\n1732: \n1733:   state_.create_chain_result_ =\n1734:       CreateChainResult(filter_chain_factory.createFilterChain(callbacks), upgrade);\n1735:   return state_.create_chain_result_;\n1736: }\n1737: \n1738: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::requestDataDrained() {\n1739:   // If this is called it means the call to requestDataTooLarge() was a\n1740:   // streaming call, or a 413 would have been sent.\n1741:   onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n1742: }\n1743: \n1744: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() {\n1745:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n1746: }\n1747: \n1748: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(\n1749:     DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) {\n1750:   // This is called exactly once per upstream-stream, by the router filter. Therefore, we\n1751:   // expect the same callbacks to not be registered twice.\n1752:   ASSERT(std::find(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.begin(), parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(),\n1753:                    &watermark_callbacks) == parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end());\n1754:   parent_.watermark_callbacks_.emplace(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(), &watermark_callbacks);\n1755:   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < parent_.high_watermark_count_; ++i) {\n1756:     watermark_callbacks.onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n1757:   }\n1758: }\n1759: \n1760: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(\n1761:     DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks& watermark_callbacks) {\n1762:   ASSERT(std::find(parent_.watermark_callbacks_.begin(), parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end(),\n1763:                    &watermark_callbacks) != parent_.watermark_callbacks_.end());\n1764:   parent_.watermark_callbacks_.remove(&watermark_callbacks);\n1765: }\n1766: \n1767: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream(const ResponseHeaderMap* headers) {\n1768:   // Because the filter's and the HCM view of if the stream has a body and if\n1769:   // the stream is complete may differ, re-check bytesReceived() to make sure\n1770:   // there was no body from the HCM's point of view.\n1771:   if (!observedEndStream()) {\n1772:     return false;\n1773:   }\n1774: \n1775:   parent_.state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1776:   parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1777:   parent_.state_.recreated_stream_ = true;\n1778: \n1779:   parent_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\n1780:       StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().InternalRedirect);\n1781: \n1782:   if (headers != nullptr) {\n1783:     // The call to setResponseHeaders is needed to ensure that the headers are properly logged in\n1784:     // access logs before the stream is destroyed. Since the function expects a\n1785:     // ResponseHeaderPtr&&, ownership of the headers must be passed. This cannot happen earlier in\n1786:     // the flow (such as in the call to setupRedirect) because at that point it is still possible\n1787:     // for the headers to be used in a different logical branch. We work around this by creating a\n1788:     // copy and passing ownership of the copy instead.\n1789:     ResponseHeaderMapPtr headers_copy = createHeaderMap<ResponseHeaderMapImpl>(*headers);\n1790:     parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseHeaders(std::move(headers_copy));\n1791:     parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.chargeStats(*headers);\n1792:   }\n1793: \n1794:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.recreateStream(parent_.streamInfo().filterState());\n1795: \n1796:   return true;\n1797: }\n1798: \n1799: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addUpstreamSocketOptions(\n1800:     const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options) {\n1801: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1500-1800"
  }
}

\n1966: uint64_t ActiveStreamFilterBase::streamId() const { return parent_.streamId(); }\n1967: \n1968: Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::account() const {\n1969:   return parent_.account();\n1970: }\n1971: \n1972: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::setUpstreamOverrideHost(\n1973:     Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost upstream_override_host) {\n1974:   parent_.upstream_override_host_.first.assign(upstream_override_host.first);\n1975:   parent_.upstream_override_host_.second = upstream_override_host.second;\n1976: }\n1977: \n1978: absl::optional<Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost>\n1979: ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::upstreamOverrideHost() const {\n1980:   if (parent_.upstream_override_host_.first.empty()) {\n1981:     return absl::nullopt;\n1982:   }\n1983:   return Upstream::LoadBalancerContext::OverrideHost{\n1984:       absl::string_view(parent_.upstream_override_host_.first),\n1985:       parent_.upstream_override_host_.second};\n1986: }\n1987: \n1988: } // namespace Http\n1989: } // namespace Envoy\n1990: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1800-2000"
  }
}
