parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::setBufferLimit(uint64_t limit) { parent_.setBufferLimit(limit); }\\n360: \\n361: uint64_t ActiveStreamFilterBase::bufferLimit() { return parent_.buffer_limit_; }\\n362: \\n363: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::sendLocalReply(\\n364:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\\n365:     std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\\n366:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\\n367:   if (!streamInfo().filterState()->hasData<LocalReplyOwnerObject>(LocalReplyFilterStateKey)) {\\n368:     streamInfo().filterState()->setData(\\n369:         LocalReplyFilterStateKey,\\n370:         std::make_shared<LocalReplyOwnerObject>(filter_context_.config_name),\\n371:         StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::ReadOnly,\\n372:         StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::FilterChain);\\n373:   }\\n374: \\n375:   parent_.sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\\n376: }\\n377: \\n378: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\\n379:   // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\\n380:   // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\\n381:   // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\\n382:   // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\\n383:   // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\\n384:   return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\\n385: }\\n386: \\n387: bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\\n388:   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\\n389:   // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\\n390:   // called, etc.\\n391:   return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain();\\n392: }\\n393: \\n394: Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\\n395:   auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\\n396:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\\n397:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\\n398:       []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\\n399:   buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\\n400:   return buffer;\\n401: }\\n402: \\n403: Buffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\\n404:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\\n405: }\\n406: \\n407: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::observedEndStream() { return parent_.decoderObservedEndStream(); }\\n408: \\n409: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doHeaders(bool end_stream) {\\n410:   parent_.decodeHeaders(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders(), end_stream);\\n411: }\\n412: \\n413: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doData(bool end_stream) {\\n414:   parent_.decodeData(this, *parent_.buffered_request_data_, end_stream,\\n415:                      FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\\n416: }\\n417: \\n418: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doTrailers() {\\n419:   parent_.decodeTrailers(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\\n420: }\\n421: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::hasTrailers() {\\n422:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers().has_value();\\n423: }\\n424: \\n425: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::drainSavedRequestMetadata() {\\n426:   ASSERT(saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr);\\n427:   for (auto& metadata_map : *getSavedRequestMetadata()) {\\n428:     parent_.decodeMetadata(this, *metadata_map);\\n429:   }\\n430:   getSavedRequestMetadata()->clear();\\n431: }\\n432: \\n433: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() {\\n434:   if (parent_.state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n435:     // The decoder filter chain has been aborted, possibly due to a local reply. In this case,\\n436:     // there's no reason to decode saved metadata.\\n437:     getSavedRequestMetadata()->clear();\\n438:     return;\\n439:   }\\n440:   // If we drain accumulated metadata, the iteration must start with the current filter.\\n441:   const bool saved_state = iterate_from_current_filter_;\\n442:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\\n443:   // If decodeHeaders() returns StopAllIteration, we should skip draining metadata, and wait\\n444:   // for doMetadata() to drain the metadata after iteration continues.\\n445:   if (!stoppedAll() && saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr && !getSavedRequestMetadata()->empty()) {\\n446:     drainSavedRequestMetadata();\\n447:   }\\n448:   // Restores the original value of iterate_from_current_filter_.\\n449:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = saved_state;\\n450: }\\n451: \\n452: RequestTrailerMap& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedTrailers() {\\n453:   return parent_.addDecodedTrailers();\\n454: }\\n455: \\n456: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) {\\n457:   parent_.addDecodedData(*this, data, streaming);\\n458: }\\n459: \\n460: MetadataMapVector& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedMetadata() {\\n461:   return parent_.addDecodedMetadata();\\n462: }\\n463: \\n464: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data,\\n465:                                                                bool end_stream) {\\n466:   if (!headers_continued_) {\\n467:     headers_continued_ = true;\\n468:     doHeaders(false);\\n469:   }\\n470:   if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\\n471:           \\\"envoy.reloadable_features.ext_proc_inject_data_with_state_update\\\")) {\\n472:     parent_.state().observed_decode_end_stream_ = end_stream;\\n473:   }\\n474:   parent_.decodeData(this, data, end_stream,\\n475:                      FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\\n476: }\\n477: \\n478: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::continueDecoding() { commonContinue(); }\\n479: const Buffer::Instance* ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::decodingBuffer() {\\n480:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_.get();\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::shouldLoadShed() const { return parent_.shouldLoadShed(); }\\n484: \\n485: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::modifyDecodingBuffer(\\n486:     std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) {\\n487:   ASSERT(parent_.state_.latest_data_decoding_filter_ == this);\\n488:   callback(*parent_.buffered_request_data_.get());\\n489: }\\n490: \\n491: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::sendLocalReply(\\n492:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\\n493:     std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\\n494:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\\n495:   ActiveStreamFilterBase::sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::sendGoAwayAndClose(bool graceful) {\\n499:   parent_.sendGoAwayAndClose(graceful);\\n500: }\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1-500\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n2: \n3: #include <functional>\n4: \n5: #include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n6: #include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n7: \n8: #include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n9: #include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n10: #include \"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n11: #include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n12: #include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n13: #include \"source/common/http/header_utility.h\"\n14: #include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n15: #include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n16: \n17: #include \"matching/data_impl.h\"\n18: \n19: namespace Envoy {\n20: namespace Http {\n21: \n22: namespace {\n23: \n24: // Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\n25: template <class Filters>\n26: void recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\n27:                             typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\n28:   // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\n29:   if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\n30:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n31:     return;\n32:   }\n33: \n34:   // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\n35:   // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\n36:   // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\n37:   // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\n38:   //\n39:   // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\n40:   // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\n41:   // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\n42:   // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\n43:   // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\n44:   if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\n45:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n46:   }\n47: }\n48: \n49: void finalizeHeaders(FilterManagerCallbacks& callbacks, StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\n50:                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n51:   const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route = stream_info.route();\n52:   if (route != nullptr && route->routeEntry() != nullptr) {\n53:     const Formatter::Context formatter_context{\n54:         callbacks.requestHeaders().ptr(), &headers, {}, {}, {}, &callbacks.activeSpan()};\n55:     route->routeEntry()->finalizeResponseHeaders(headers, formatter_context, stream_info);\n56:   }\n57: }\n58: \n59: } // namespace\n60: \n61: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {\n62:   if (!canContinue()) {\n63:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n64:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n65:     return;\n66:   }\n67: \n68:   // Set ScopeTrackerScopeState if there's no existing crash context.\n69:   ScopeTrackedObjectStack encapsulated_object;\n70:   absl::optional<ScopeTrackerScopeState> state;\n71:   if (parent_.dispatcher_.trackedObjectStackIsEmpty()) {\n72:     restoreContextOnContinue(encapsulated_object);\n73:     state.emplace(&encapsulated_object, parent_.dispatcher_);\n74:   }\n75: \n76:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"continuing filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n77:                    static_cast<const void*>(this));\n78:   ASSERT(!canIterate(),\n79:          \"Attempting to continue iteration while the IterationState is already Continue\");\n80:   // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, set iterate_from_current_filter_ to true so the\n81:   // filter iteration starts with the current filter instead of the next one.\n82:   if (stoppedAll()) {\n83:     iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\n84:   }\n85:   allowIteration();\n86: \n87:   // Only resume with do1xxHeaders() if we've actually seen 1xx headers.\n88:   if (has1xxHeaders()) {\n89:     continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n90:     do1xxHeaders();\n91:     // If the response headers have not yet come in, don't continue on with\n92:     // headers and body. doHeaders expects request headers to exist.\n93:     if (!parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders()) {\n94:       return;\n95:     }\n96:   }\n97: \n98:   if (!canContinue()) {\n99:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n100:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n101:     return;\n102:   }\n103: \n104:   // Make sure that we handle the zero byte data frame case. We make no effort to optimize this\n105:   // case in terms of merging it into a header only request/response. This could be done in the\n106:   // future.\n107:   if (!headers_continued_) {\n108:     headers_continued_ = true;\n109:     doHeaders(observedEndStream() && !bufferedData() && !hasTrailers());\n110:   }\n111: \n112:   if (!canContinue()) {\n113:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n114:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n115:     return;\n116:   }\n117: \n118:   doMetadata();\n119: \n120:   if (!canContinue()) {\n121:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n122:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n123:     return;\n124:   }\n125: \n126:   // It is possible for trailers to be added during doData(). doData() itself handles continuation\n127:   // of trailers for the non-continuation case. Thus, we must keep track of whether we had\n128:   // trailers prior to calling doData(). If we do, then we continue them here, otherwise we rely\n129:   // on doData() to do so.\n130:   const bool had_trailers_before_data = hasTrailers();\n131:   if (bufferedData()) {\n132:     doData(observedEndStream() && !had_trailers_before_data);\n133:   }\n134: \n135:   if (!canContinue()) {\n136:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n137:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\n138:     return;\n139:   }\n140: \n141:   if (had_trailers_before_data) {\n142:     doTrailers();\n143:   }\n144: \n145:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = false;\n146: }\n147: \n148: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status) {\n149:   ASSERT(parent_.state_.has_1xx_headers_);\n150:   ASSERT(!continued_1xx_headers_);\n151:   ASSERT(canIterate());\n152: \n153:   switch (status) {\n154:   case Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue:\n155:     continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n156:     return true;\n157:   case Filter1xxHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n158:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n159:     return false;\n160:   }\n161: \n162:   PANIC_DUE_TO_CORRUPT_ENUM;\n163: }\n164: \n165: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status,\n166:                                                               bool& end_stream) {\n167:   ASSERT(!headers_continued_);\n168:   ASSERT(canIterate());\n169: \n170:   switch (status) {\n171:   case FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n172:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n173:     break;\n174:   case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer:\n175:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllBuffer;\n176:     break;\n177:   case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark:\n178:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n179:     break;\n180:   case FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream:\n181:     end_stream = false;\n182:     headers_continued_ = true;\n183:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"converting to headers and body (body not available yet)\", parent_);\n184:     break;\n185:   case FilterHeadersStatus::Continue:\n186:     headers_continued_ = true;\n187:     break;\n188:   }\n189: \n190:   handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback();\n191: \n192:   if (stoppedAll() || status == FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration) {\n193:     return false;\n194:   } else {\n195:     return true;\n196:   }\n197: }\n198: \n199: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleBufferData(Buffer::Instance& provided_data) {\n200: \n201:   // The way we do buffering is a little complicated which is why we have this common function\n202:   // which is used for both encoding and decoding. When data first comes into our filter pipeline,\n203:   // we send it through. Any filter can choose to stop iteration and buffer or not. If we then\n204:   // continue iteration in the future, we use the buffered data. A future filter can stop and\n205:   // buffer again. In this case, since we are already operating on buffered data, we don't\n206:   // rebuffer, because we assume the filter has modified the buffer as it wishes in place.\n207:   if (bufferedData().get() != &provided_data) {\n208:     if (!bufferedData()) {\n209:       bufferedData() = createBuffer();\n210:     }\n211:     bufferedData()->move(provided_data);\n212:   }\n213: }\n214: \n215: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterDataCallback(FilterDataStatus status,\n216:                                                            Buffer::Instance& provided_data,\n217:                                                            bool& buffer_was_streaming) {\n218: \n219:   if (status == FilterDataStatus::Continue) {\n220:     if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\n221:       commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\n222:       commonContinue();\n223:       return false;\n224:     } else {\n225:       ASSERT(headers_continued_);\n226:     }\n227:   } else {\n228:     iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n229:     if (status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer ||\n230:         status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark) {\n231:       buffer_was_streaming = status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark;\n232:       commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\n233:     } else if (observedEndStream() && !hasTrailers() && !bufferedData() &&\n234:                // If the stream is destroyed, no need to handle the data buffer or trailers.\n235:                // This can occur if the filter calls sendLocalReply.\n236:                !parent_.state_.destroyed_) {\n237:       // If this filter is doing StopIterationNoBuffer and this stream is terminated with a zero\n238:       // byte data frame, we need to create an empty buffer to make sure that when commonContinue\n239:       // is called, the pipeline resumes with an empty data frame with end_stream = true\n240:       ASSERT(end_stream_);\n241:       bufferedData() = createBuffer();\n242:     }\n243: \n244:     return false;\n245:   }\n246: \n247:   return true;\n248: }\n249: \n250: bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback(FilterTrailersStatus status) {\n251: \n252:   if (status == FilterTrailersStatus::Continue) {\n253:     if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\n254:       commonContinue();\n255:       return false;\n256:     } else {\n257:       ASSERT(headers_continued_);\n258:     }\n259:   } else if (status == FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration) {\n260:     if (canIterate()) {\n261:       iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n262:     }\n263:     return false;\n264:   }\n265: \n266:   return true;\n267: }\n268: \n269: OptRef<const Network::Connection> ActiveStreamFilterBase::connection() {\n270:   return parent_.connection();\n271: }\n272: \n273: Event::Dispatcher& ActiveStreamFilterBase::dispatcher() { return parent_.dispatcher_; }\n274: \n275: StreamInfo::StreamInfo& ActiveStreamFilterBase::streamInfo() { return parent_.streamInfo(); }\n276: \n277: Tracing::Span& ActiveStreamFilterBase::activeSpan() {\n278:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.activeSpan();\n279: }\n280: \n281: const ScopeTrackedObject& ActiveStreamFilterBase::scope() {\n282:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.scope();\n283: }\n284: \n285: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::restoreContextOnContinue(\n286:     ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) {\n287:   parent_.contextOnContinue(tracked_object_stack);\n288: }\n289: \n290: OptRef<const Tracing::Config> ActiveStreamFilterBase::tracingConfig() const {\n291:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.tracingConfig();\n292: }\n293: \n294: Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr ActiveStreamFilterBase::clusterInfo() {\n295:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.clusterInfo();\n296: }\n297: \n298: Router::RouteConstSharedPtr ActiveStreamFilterBase::route() { return getRoute(); }\n299: \n300: Router::RouteConstSharedPtr ActiveStreamFilterBase::getRoute() const {\n301:   if (parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks()) {\n302:     return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks()->route(nullptr);\n303:   }\n304:   return parent_.streamInfo().route();\n305: }\n306: \n307: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::resetIdleTimer() {\n308:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n309: }\n310: \n311: const Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig*\n312: ActiveStreamFilterBase::mostSpecificPerFilterConfig() const {\n313:   auto current_route = getRoute();\n314:   if (current_route == nullptr) {\n315:     return nullptr;\n316:   }\n317:   return current_route->mostSpecificPerFilterConfig(filter_context_.config_name);\n318: }\n319: \n320: Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigs ActiveStreamFilterBase::perFilterConfigs() const {\n321:   Router::RouteConstSharedPtr current_route = getRoute();\n322:   if (current_route == nullptr) {\n323:     return {};\n324:   }\n325: \n326:   return current_route->perFilterConfigs(filter_context_.config_name);\n327: }\n328: \n329: Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::http1StreamEncoderOptions() {\n330:   // TODO(mattklein123): At some point we might want to actually wrap this interface but for now\n331:   // we give the filter direct access to the encoder options.\n332:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.http1StreamEncoderOptions();\n333: }\n334: \n335: OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> ActiveStreamFilterBase::downstreamCallbacks() {\n336:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks();\n337: }\n338: \n339: OptRef<UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> ActiveStreamFilterBase::upstreamCallbacks() {\n340:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.upstreamCallbacks();\n341: }\n342: \n343: RequestHeaderMapOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::requestHeaders() {\n344:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders();\n345: }\n346: RequestTrailerMapOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::requestTrailers() {\n347:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers();\n348: }\n349: ResponseHeaderMapOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::informationalHeaders() {\n350:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders();\n351: }\n352: ResponseHeaderMapOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::responseHeaders() {\n353:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders();\n354: }\n355: ResponseTrailerMapOptRef ActiveStreamFilterBase::responseTrailers() {\n356:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\n357: }\n358: \n359: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::setBufferLimit(uint64_t limit) { parent_.setBufferLimit(limit); }\n360: \n361: uint64_t ActiveStreamFilterBase::bufferLimit() { return parent_.buffer_limit_; }\n362: \n363: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::sendLocalReply(\n364:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\n365:     std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n366:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n367:   if (!streamInfo().filterState()->hasData<LocalReplyOwnerObject>(LocalReplyFilterStateKey)) {\n368:     streamInfo().filterState()->setData(\n369:         LocalReplyFilterStateKey,\n370:         std::make_shared<LocalReplyOwnerObject>(filter_context_.config_name),\n371:         StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::ReadOnly,\n372:         StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::FilterChain);\n373:   }\n374: \n375:   parent_.sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\n376: }\n377: \n378: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n379:   // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\n380:   // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\n381:   // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\n382:   // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\n383:   // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\n384:   return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\n385: }\n386: \n387: bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n388:   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n389:   // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n390:   // called, etc.\n391:   return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain();\n392: }\n393: \n394: Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n395:   auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n396:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\n397:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\n398:       []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\n399:   buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\n400:   return buffer;\n401: }\n402: \n403: Buffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\n404:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\n405: }\n406: \n407: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::observedEndStream() { return parent_.decoderObservedEndStream(); }\n408: \n409: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doHeaders(bool end_stream) {\n410:   parent_.decodeHeaders(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders(), end_stream);\n411: }\n412: \n413: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doData(bool end_stream) {\n414:   parent_.decodeData(this, *parent_.buffered_request_data_, end_stream,\n415:                      FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n416: }\n417: \n418: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doTrailers() {\n419:   parent_.decodeTrailers(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\n420: }\n421: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::hasTrailers() {\n422:   return parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers().has_value();\n423: }\n424: \n425: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::drainSavedRequestMetadata() {\n426:   ASSERT(saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr);\n427:   for (auto& metadata_map : *getSavedRequestMetadata()) {\n428:     parent_.decodeMetadata(this, *metadata_map);\n429:   }\n430:   getSavedRequestMetadata()->clear();\n431: }\n432: \n433: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() {\n434:   if (parent_.state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n435:     // The decoder filter chain has been aborted, possibly due to a local reply. In this case,\n436:     // there's no reason to decode saved metadata.\n437:     getSavedRequestMetadata()->clear();\n438:     return;\n439:   }\n440:   // If we drain accumulated metadata, the iteration must start with the current filter.\n441:   const bool saved_state = iterate_from_current_filter_;\n442:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\n443:   // If decodeHeaders() returns StopAllIteration, we should skip draining metadata, and wait\n444:   // for doMetadata() to drain the metadata after iteration continues.\n445:   if (!stoppedAll() && saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr && !getSavedRequestMetadata()->empty()) {\n446:     drainSavedRequestMetadata();\n447:   }\n448:   // Restores the original value of iterate_from_current_filter_.\n449:   iterate_from_current_filter_ = saved_state;\n450: }\n451: \n452: RequestTrailerMap& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedTrailers() {\n453:   return parent_.addDecodedTrailers();\n454: }\n455: \n456: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) {\n457:   parent_.addDecodedData(*this, data, streaming);\n458: }\n459: \n460: MetadataMapVector& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedMetadata() {\n461:   return parent_.addDecodedMetadata();\n462: }\n463: \n464: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data,\n465:                                                                bool end_stream) {\n466:   if (!headers_continued_) {\n467:     headers_continued_ = true;\n468:     doHeaders(false);\n469:   }\n470:   if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n471:           \"envoy.reloadable_features.ext_proc_inject_data_with_state_update\")) {\n472:     parent_.state().observed_decode_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n473:   }\n474:   parent_.decodeData(this, data, end_stream,\n475:                      FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n476: }\n477: \n478: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::continueDecoding() { commonContinue(); }\n479: const Buffer::Instance* ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::decodingBuffer() {\n480:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_.get();\n481: }\n482: \n483: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::shouldLoadShed() const { return parent_.shouldLoadShed(); }\n484: \n485: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::modifyDecodingBuffer(\n486:     std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) {\n487:   ASSERT(parent_.state_.latest_data_decoding_filter_ == this);\n488:   callback(*parent_.buffered_request_data_.get());\n489: }\n490: \n491: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::sendLocalReply(\n492:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\n493:     std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n494:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n495:   ActiveStreamFilterBase::sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\n496: }\n497: \n498: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::sendGoAwayAndClose(bool graceful) {\n499:   parent_.sendGoAwayAndClose(graceful);\n500: }\n501: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1-500"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"900:     }\\n901:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeMetadata;\\n902:     FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeMetadata(metadata_map);\\n903:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeMetadata;\\n904: \\n905:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"decode metadata called: filter={} status={}, metadata: {}\\\", *this,\\n906:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\\n907:                      metadata_map);\\n908:     if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n909:       // If the decoder filter chain has been aborted, then either:\\n910:       // 1. This filter has sent a local reply or GoAway from decode metadata.\\n911:       // 2. This filter is the terminal http filter, and an upstream HTTP filter has sent a local\\n912:       // reply.\\n913:       ASSERT((status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) ||\\n914:              (std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end()));\\n915:       executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\\n916:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n917:                        \\\"decodeMetadata filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n918:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n919:       return;\\n920:     }\\n921: \\n922:     // Add the processed metadata to the next entry.\\n923:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\\n924:       if (std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\\n925:         (*std::next(entry))\\n926:             ->getSavedRequestMetadata()\\n927:             ->emplace_back(std::make_unique<MetadataMap>(metadata_map));\\n928:       }\\n929:       (*entry)->commonContinue();\\n930:       return;\\n931:     }\\n932:   }\\n933: }\\n934: \\n935: void FilterManager::disarmRequestTimeout() { filter_manager_callbacks_.disarmRequestTimeout(); }\\n936: \\n937: StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator\\n938: FilterManager::commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,\\n939:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\\n940:   // Only do base state setting on the initial call. Subsequent calls for filtering do not touch\\n941:   // the base state.\\n942:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"commonEncodePrefix end_stream: {}, isHalfCloseEnabled: {}\\\", *this,\\n943:                    end_stream, filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\\n944:   if (filter == nullptr) {\\n945:     if (end_stream) {\\n946:       ASSERT(!state_.observed_encode_end_stream_);\\n947:       state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\\n948: \\n949:       // When half close semantics are disabled, receiving end stream from the upstream causes\\n950:       // decoder filter to stop, as neither filters nor upstream is interested in downstream data.\\n951:       // half close is enabled in case tcp proxying is done with http1 encoder. In this case, we\\n952:       // should not stop decoder filter chain when end_stream is true, as it will cause any data\\n953:       // sent in the upstream direction to be\\n954:       // dropped.\\n955:       if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\\n956:         state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n957:       }\\n958:     }\\n959:     return encoder_filters_.begin();\\n960:   }\\n961: \\n962:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\\n963:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\\n964:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\\n965:     // The current filter's encoding callback has not be called. Call it now.\\n966:     return filter->entry();\\n967:   }\\n968:   return std::next(filter->entry());\\n969: }\\n970: \\n971: StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator\\n972: FilterManager::commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,\\n973:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\\n974:   if (!filter) {\\n975:     return decoder_filters_.begin();\\n976:   }\\n977:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\\n978:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\\n979:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\\n980:     // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\\n981:     return filter->entry();\\n982:   }\\n983:   return std::next(filter->entry());\\n984: }\\n985: \\n986: void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\\n987:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\\n988:   filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\\n989: \\n990:   for (auto entry : filters_) {\\n991:     if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\\n992:       data.reset_imminent_ = true;\\n993:     }\\n994:   }\\n995:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\\n996: }\\n997: \\n998: void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\\n999:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\\n1000:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL900-1000\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "900:     }\n901:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeMetadata;\n902:     FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeMetadata(metadata_map);\n903:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeMetadata;\n904: \n905:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode metadata called: filter={} status={}, metadata: {}\", *this,\n906:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\n907:                      metadata_map);\n908:     if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n909:       // If the decoder filter chain has been aborted, then either:\n910:       // 1. This filter has sent a local reply or GoAway from decode metadata.\n911:       // 2. This filter is the terminal http filter, and an upstream HTTP filter has sent a local\n912:       // reply.\n913:       ASSERT((status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) ||\n914:              (std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end()));\n915:       executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n916:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n917:                        \"decodeMetadata filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n918:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n919:       return;\n920:     }\n921: \n922:     // Add the processed metadata to the next entry.\n923:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\n924:       if (std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n925:         (*std::next(entry))\n926:             ->getSavedRequestMetadata()\n927:             ->emplace_back(std::make_unique<MetadataMap>(metadata_map));\n928:       }\n929:       (*entry)->commonContinue();\n930:       return;\n931:     }\n932:   }\n933: }\n934: \n935: void FilterManager::disarmRequestTimeout() { filter_manager_callbacks_.disarmRequestTimeout(); }\n936: \n937: StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator\n938: FilterManager::commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,\n939:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n940:   // Only do base state setting on the initial call. Subsequent calls for filtering do not touch\n941:   // the base state.\n942:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"commonEncodePrefix end_stream: {}, isHalfCloseEnabled: {}\", *this,\n943:                    end_stream, filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\n944:   if (filter == nullptr) {\n945:     if (end_stream) {\n946:       ASSERT(!state_.observed_encode_end_stream_);\n947:       state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\n948: \n949:       // When half close semantics are disabled, receiving end stream from the upstream causes\n950:       // decoder filter to stop, as neither filters nor upstream is interested in downstream data.\n951:       // half close is enabled in case tcp proxying is done with http1 encoder. In this case, we\n952:       // should not stop decoder filter chain when end_stream is true, as it will cause any data\n953:       // sent in the upstream direction to be\n954:       // dropped.\n955:       if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n956:         state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n957:       }\n958:     }\n959:     return encoder_filters_.begin();\n960:   }\n961: \n962:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n963:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n964:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n965:     // The current filter's encoding callback has not be called. Call it now.\n966:     return filter->entry();\n967:   }\n968:   return std::next(filter->entry());\n969: }\n970: \n971: StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator\n972: FilterManager::commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,\n973:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n974:   if (!filter) {\n975:     return decoder_filters_.begin();\n976:   }\n977:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n978:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n979:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n980:     // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\n981:     return filter->entry();\n982:   }\n983:   return std::next(filter->entry());\n984: }\n985: \n986: void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\n987:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\n988:   filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\n989: \n990:   for (auto entry : filters_) {\n991:     if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\n992:       data.reset_imminent_ = true;\n993:     }\n994:   }\n995:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\n996: }\n997: \n998: void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n999:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\n1000:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n1001: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL900-1000"
  }
}
35. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
